The six existing Reichenbach Boolean predicates are then recharacterized as `Domain.relatedByName` queries against named atom-sets from the Allen algebra: `isPast_iff_relatedByName` (`R precedes P`), `isFuture_iff_relatedByName` (`P precedes R`), `isPresent_iff_relatedByName` (`R equal P`), `isPerfect_iff_relatedByName` (`E precedes R`), `isProspective_iff_relatedByName` (`R precedes E`), `isPerfective_iff_relatedByName` (`E equal R`, for the point-time approximation). Each iff is proved via two private helpers `point_precedes_iff`/`point_equal_iff` (point intervals at `s, t` ↔ `s < t` / `s = t`), which let the bridges close in ~10 lines: forward direction supplies witnesses via `toDomain_find*`, backward direction extracts equalities via `Option.some.inj` and uses the helpers. New `equalSet : List AllenRelation := [.equal]` added to `Allen.lean` alongside the other named atom-sets — needed for the present/perfective bridges, which unlike past/future are equality (rather than precedence) predicates. Step 4 is **additive** (matching Step 2's philosophy): the `ReichenbachFrame` structure and all six Boolean predicates are unchanged, and all 28 downstream files using `ReichenbachFrame.referenceTime`/`isPast`/etc. continue to build clean (verified: `Core.Temporal.Tense`, `Theories.Semantics.Tense.{Basic,Compositional,SequenceOfTense}` all build through the bridge, 898 jobs). Domain-level tooling can now work uniformly with `f.toDomain.relatedByName precedesSet "R" "P"` while existing call sites continue with `f.isPast`. The bridge theorems make explicit that Reichenbach's tense/aspect predicates are *exactly* the singleton-atom Allen projections on point intervals — what's special about Reichenbach 1947 is the role-set commitment (`["S", "P", "R", "E"]`), not the underlying relation algebra.

## [0.229.940] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mathlib-PR polish pass over the Phase 1–4 Case-API work.** `Theories/Syntax/Case/Licensing.lean`: (1) `LicensedNP` now `extends Syntax.Case.NPInDomain` rather than duplicating fields, with `needsLicensing : Bool` modeling Pesetsky-Torrego unvalued [Case]; new `LicensedNP.caseFeature : Option Core.Case` returns `none` exactly when the NP is active for licensing, with `caseFeature_none_iff_active` proving the equivalence. (2) `LicensingOutcome` constructors now carry the licenser identity and assigned case (`byPrimary head c`, `bySecondary head c`, `byLexical c`, `unlicensed`) so the outcome is informative rather than an opaque tag; new `LicensingOutcome.isLicensed` and `assignedCase` projections. (3) `licenseActive` now actually uses the primary head/case (the `_primary` parameter was dead before). (4) `licenseSecondaries` hoisted to a private top-level `def` (was a where-clause), enabling reuse and induction. (5) New § 6 of property theorems: `licenseSecondaries_length` (no NP dropped), `licenseActive_length`/`licenseNPs_length` (algorithm preserves NP count), `licenseNPs_labels` (label preservation by `List.map` construction, not by length-bookkeeping), `first_active_byPrimary` (the first active NP always gets the primary), `nonNeeding_isLicensed_byPrimary` (NPs without `needsLicensing` are licensed by primary, not secondary — Kalin Thesis 1), `licenseActive_no_crash_when_enough_secondaries` (Kalin Thesis 2), `no_need_means_no_secondaries_used` (Kalin Thesis 3 — DOM signature is empty when no NP requires licensing). (6) New § 7 bridge to `Minimalism.CaseFilter`: `LicensingOutcome.toDPFeatures` projects to the Minimalist DP feature record; `isLicensed_iff_satisfiesCaseFilter` (per-NP equivalence) and `all_isLicensed_iff_caseFilterHolds` (clause-level equivalence) make the Kalin licensing API and the Chomsky Case Filter observably the same predicate. (7) `byPrimary` now records the primary's head string so `primary_head_distinguishes` can prove that licensing identity is load-bearing (changing `primary.head` from `"T"` to `"v"` flips the outcome). (8) §8 worked examples migrated from `native_decide` to `decide` per the proof-style preference. (9) Docstring hedge: "secondaries can be analyzed as conditional voice-style heads" reframed as a description of one possible mapping rather than a claim Kalin makes. `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`: 19 `native_decide` invocations migrated to `decide` (all are concrete finite-data checks). `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Scott2023.lean`: 2 `native_decide` invocations migrated to `decide`. `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Caha2009.lean`: new § 3 cross-inventory survey — `cahaSurvey : List (String × Bool)` bundles the conformance verdict for all 22 formalized Fragment case inventories into one finite table; `cahaSurvey_length` (= 22, total coverage), `all_inventories_classified` (the violator set is exactly `[("dargwa", false), ("finnish", false)]`), `caha_conformance_count` (20/22 conform). The survey breaks if a new Fragment is added without classification, providing the maintenance contract the per-language theorems alone don't guarantee. Build clean for all Case-related files (925 jobs in the closure of `Licensing.lean ∪ Dependent.lean ∪ Scott2023.lean ∪ Caha2009.lean ∪ Marantz1991.lean ∪ Baker2015.lean ∪ Woolford1997.lean ∪ Pesetsky2013.lean ∪ CaseContainment.lean ∪ CaseFilter.lean`). Unrelated concurrent-session breakage in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Ordering.lean` (`Core/Order/Satisfaction.lean` API drift) is left for that session to resolve per the no-blanket-revert rule.

## [0.229.939] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 3 of the Tense-API redesign: `Core/Temporal/Domain.lean` — temporal domains (Declerck 2006).** New foundational module formalizing the shared substrate of @cite{declerck-2006}'s temporal domains and @cite{reichenbach-1947}'s S/P/R/E framework. Three core types: `abbrev TO (Time : Type*) [LE Time] := Interval Time` (a *time of orientation*: realized as `Interval Time` so the same type covers both Reichenbach's point times via `TO.point t = Interval.point t` and Declerck's extended times-of-situation via general intervals); `structure NamedTO Time` (a TO with an identifying string label, enabling role-based access without baking a fixed role-list into the type — `"S"`/`"P"`/`"R"`/`"E"` for Reichenbach, `"T₀"`/`"T-Past"`/etc. for Declerck); `structure Domain Time` (central `NamedTO` + list of sub-`NamedTO`s — covers both Reichenbach's 4-role layout and Declerck's variable-count nested TOs). Two non-obvious design choices are documented in the `Domain` docstring: (1) **no relation storage** — TOs are intervals on a `LinearOrder`, so their Allen relation is determined by the order; storing relations would either be redundant or contradictory, so we compute on demand via Step 2's `Interval.allenRel`; (2) **NamedTO list, not record** — different domain-shaped theories use different role-sets, so a label-keyed list keeps `Domain` framework-agnostic and lets downstream wrappers commit to fixed role-sets on top. Five Domain accessors: `all` (central first, then sub-TOs), `find?` (look up by label, returns `Option (TO Time)`), `labels` (list of all role labels — useful for stating role-set invariants), `noncomputable allenRel` (lifted projection function), `relatedBy` (two TOs stand in some atom of `S` — lifts `AllenRelation.holdsIn` to the domain level), `relatedByName` (lookup-and-relate: do the two named TOs both exist in the domain and stand in some atom of `S`? Phrases tense predicates like "R precedes P" against a domain by name). Two `@[simp]` membership lemmas: `all_central` (the central TO is in `all`), `all_sub` (sub-TOs are in `all`). Reichenbach builders for the canonical S/P/R/E role-set: `reichenbachLabels := ["S", "P", "R", "E"]` (the four canonical Reichenbach role labels, extended with @cite{kiparsky-2002}'s perspective time P), `ofReichenbachPoints S P R E : Domain Time` (build a domain from four point times), plus five `@[simp]` `rfl`-theorems (`ofReichenbachPoints_labels` + `_findS`/`_findP`/`_findR`/`_findE`) — Step 4's `ReichenbachFrame ↔ Domain` bridge will use these. The Domain module is **substrate**: it does not add tense semantics (reserved for `Tense.lean`), aspect (reserved for `Theories/Semantics/Tense/Aspect/`), or sector classification (reserved for `DeclercianSchema` in Step 5 — the `Zone` enum lives there because it's Declerck-specific). Wired into `Linglib.lean` between `Allen` and `Reichenbach`. Build clean (866 jobs).

## [0.229.938] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 2 of the Tense-API redesign: `Interval` predicates restated as Allen projections.** Adds an "Atom Sets and `holdsIn`" section to `Core/Temporal/Allen.lean` introducing `def AllenRelation.holdsIn (S : List AllenRelation) (i j : Interval Time) : Prop := ∃ r ∈ S, r.holds i j` — the uniform "at least one of these atoms holds" predicate. Three structural lemmas: `holdsIn_nil` (empty set ↔ False, `@[simp]`), `holdsIn_singleton` (singleton ↔ holds, `@[simp]`), `holdsIn_cons` (cons ↔ disjunction), `holdsIn_mono` (subset monotonicity: enlarging the atom set weakens the predicate). Eight named atom-sets corresponding to existing `Interval` predicates: `precedesSet := [.precedes]`, `meetsSet := [.meets]`, `beforeSet := [.precedes, .meets]` (the conflation `i.finish ≤ j.start` represents), `afterSet := [.precededBy, .metBy]`, `containmentSet := [.starts, .equal, .finishes, .during]` (subinterval), `properContainmentSet := [.starts, .finishes, .during]` (excludes `equal`), `finalContainmentSet := [.finishes, .equal]`, `reverseContainmentSet := [.startedBy, .equal, .finishedBy, .contains]` (reverse direction of `subinterval`), `overlapSet` (the eleven atoms compatible with sharing at least one moment — everything except `precedes`/`precededBy`). The existing `_iff_allen` bridge theorems refactored to use `holdsIn` consistently — including a new `properSubinterval_iff_allen` not covered before — making the algebraic structure (`S₁ ⊆ S₂ ⇒ holdsIn S₁ ⇒ holdsIn S₂`) explicit. The **projection function** `Interval.allenRel : Interval Time → Interval Time → AllenRelation` (noncomputable, via `Classical.choose` over `holds_exists`) — the inverse direction of `holds`, lifting an interval pair to its unique Allen atom. Three characterization theorems: `allenRel_holds` (the projected atom does hold), `allenRel_unique` (for non-degenerate intervals, every witness equals the projection — the well-definedness theorem justifying treating `allenRel i j` as **the** Allen relation), `allenRel_mem_iff_holdsIn` (the projection lands in the named atom-set iff the corresponding `holdsIn` predicate holds, on non-degenerate intervals — bridges the predicate API to the projection API). Together with `holds_exists` + `holds_unique`, this gives a full abstraction-and-projection layer over the Allen algebra: `holdsIn` lets you write predicates as named atom-sets without committing to a specific Boolean combination; `allenRel` lets you project an interval pair down to a unique atom for case analysis. Step 2 is **additive** — no existing definitions changed, no downstream call sites affected (verified: only `Linglib.lean` imports Allen.lean directly; downstream `MaximalInformativity`, `TemporalDecomposition`, `Koev2017`, `SubintervalProperty` all build clean). Build clean (865 jobs).

## [0.229.937] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Move `Core/Logic/SatisfactionOrdering.lean` → `Core/Order/Satisfaction.lean`.** The construction (a `satisfies : α → Criterion → Bool` plus criteria list inducing a preorder on `α` with `best`/`undominated` selectors) is mathematically an *order construction*, not a logical one — mathlib organizes by the structure being defined, not by what's being ordered. Moves it next to its siblings `Core/Order/Normality.lean` (`NormalityOrder`) and `Core/Order/Plausibility.lean` (`PlausibilityOrder`), and adopts their naming convention: file name drops the redundant "Ordering" suffix (the `Core/Order/` directory contextualizes it), while the structure name `SatisfactionOrdering` is preserved for self-description at call sites. Namespace `Core.SatisfactionOrdering` → `Core.Order` (matching `Core.Order.NormalityOrder`). Consumers updated: `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Ordering.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Desire.lean`, `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/Constraints/NumericalExpressions.lean` — each now uses `open Core.Order (SatisfactionOrdering)`. The `Criterion` type parameter is preserved (genuinely exercised: `cumminsOrdering : SatisfactionOrdering ViolationProfile NumeralConstraint` uses an enum criterion type, and `worldSatisfactionOrdering` in Desire.lean uses `BProp` criteria — the parameter is not vestigial). My migration files all build clean (`lake build Linglib.Core.Order.Satisfaction Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Modality.Kratzer.Ordering Linglib.Theories.Pragmatics.Implicature.Constraints.NumericalExpressions` succeeds in 872 jobs). `Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Modality.Desire` builds correctly through the migration but the full closure is currently blocked by an unrelated pre-existing break in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Flavor.lean` (concurrent-session Bool→Prop migration of `KratzerTheory.duality` left a partial proof — out of scope for this rename).

## [0.229.936] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Phase 4 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: `Theories/Syntax/Case/Licensing.lean` — @cite{kalin-2018}'s hybrid licensing framework.** New bib entry `kalin-2018` ("Licensing and Differential Object Marking: The View from Neo-Aramaic", *Syntax* 21:2, 112–159, DOI 10.1111/synt.12153, validated against the PDF). The theory file formalizes Kalin's two-parameter system: (i) `LicenserKind` distinguishes obligatory `primary` licensers (always merged, e.g., T) from `secondary` last-resort licensers (merging only when their inactivity would crash the derivation, e.g., AGRO, P-DOM); (ii) `LicensedNP.needsLicensing` parameterizes which nominals require licensing, with `lexicalCase : Option Case` independently bleeding the requirement (P/V pre-licenses). `ClauseLicensers` bundles a primary + a list of secondaries with a definitional invariant (`primary_is_primary : primary.kind = .primary := by rfl`) so the typeclass system enforces the "exactly one primary" constraint. The licensing algorithm (`licenseActive` + helper `licenseRest`) greedily activates secondaries from the queue as remaining active NPs require — modeling the global last-resort economy condition. `LicensingOutcome` records *how* each NP was licensed (`.byPrimary` / `.bySecondary head` / `.byLexical case` / `.unlicensed`); `isDOMMarked` extracts the DOM signature: marked objects are exactly those licensed by a secondary licenser. Five smoke-test theorems on a Turkish-style clause (T primary + AGRO secondary): `specific_object_is_dom_marked` (specific object → AGRO secondary), `nonspecific_object_unmarked` (no licensing need → primary suffices, no DOM), `subject_always_primary` (subject takes the primary in both clauses), `dom_signature` (DOM-marked NP list = `["obj-specific"]` vs `[]`), `no_secondary_means_crash` (clause without secondaries cannot license a second active NP). Docstring leads with the two-parameter framework, traces the relation to dependent case (largely orthogonal — both can coexist), voice-based case (secondaries can be analyzed as conditional voice-style heads), and explicitly notes @cite{coon-keine-2021}'s feature-gluttony alternative as an *adjacent* debate (Coon-Keine reject licensing as the source of *hierarchy effects* like PCC, but their account doesn't target DOM, so the two are not competing analyses of the same data). Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `Theories.Syntax.Case.Dependent`. `Dependent.lean`'s "Companion Infrastructure" section updated to cross-reference `Licensing.lean`. Build clean (289 jobs in the dependency closure of the new file).

### Changed
- **Phase 3 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: reframed `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`'s module docstring.** Stripped the embedded "Key Application: @cite{ozaki-2026}" subsection (study-specific content in a theory file = anti-pattern; the actual Ozaki content lives in `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Ozaki2026.lean`). Removed the inline `Fragments.Mayan.Mam.Agreement` cross-reference (now superseded by the three Scott2023 study files). New "Where to Find What" section enumerates the seven Case study files (`Marantz1991`, `Baker2015`, `BakerVinokurova2010`, `Woolford1997`, `Scott2023`, `Ozaki2026`, `Caha2009`) with one-line descriptions tying each to its empirical contribution. New "Companion Infrastructure" section cross-references `Voice.lean`, `CaseFilter.lean`, `Licensing.lean` (Phase 4), and `CaseContainment.lean` so a reader landing in `Dependent.lean` can navigate the surrounding Case-API surface. Theorem and definition content unchanged. The §8/§9 Ozaki-specific worked derivations in the file body remain in place pending a follow-up move to `Studies/Ozaki2026.lean`.

## [0.229.935] - 2026-04-18

- **Phase 2 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: dissolved `Phenomena/Case/Compare.lean` into `Studies/Scott2023.lean` + theory-level coverage.** The three-way comparison file (Agree vs. Dependent vs. Voice-based case) had no single-paper provenance and mixed Scott 2023's novel Voice-based contribution with theorems already covered elsewhere. Promoted the Voice-based content to a real study file `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Scott2023.lean` (namespace `Phenomena.Case.Studies.Scott2023`) — the case-theoretic counterpart to the existing `Phenomena/Agreement/Studies/Scott2023.lean` (Agree/Spellout pipeline) and `Phenomena/Ergativity/Studies/Scott2023.lean` (super-extended ergativity). Three sections: § 1 Voice's positional case assignment (`voice_assigns_case_by_position`, `voice_based_tripartite` — three distinct cases from three different heads via the Mam fragment's `MamArgPosition.case`), § 2 contrast with Agree-based case (`agree_anticausative_not_phase`, `agree_voice_is_phase_head` — Agree ties ACC to phase-hood, Voice-based assignment doesn't), § 3 contrast with dependent case (`dependent_case_ignores_voice` — same ACC for unaccusative-vs-transitive themes; `dependent_case_tripartite` — same surface pattern via positional rather than θ-role grounds). Docstring leads with @cite{scott-2023}'s three-heads/three-cases architecture, traces the inherent-ERG move back to @cite{woolford-1997}, cross-references the sister Scott2023 study files. The remaining Compare.lean theorems were all duplicative: § 1's `agree_predicts_no_acc_for_unaccusatives` = `Voice.lean:177` `anticausative_not_phase`; § 2's `single_np_gets_nom`/`transitive_acc` = `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`'s `single_np_nom` and `tripartite_three_distinct`/`no_voice_needed_for_acc`; § 5's `case_filter_example`/`dependent_case_always_assigns` subsumed by `CaseFilter.lean`'s structural `case_filter_at_interfaces` and `caseFilterHolds` definition. `Linglib.lean` import swapped (Compare removed, Scott2023 added). Build clean.

## [0.229.934] - 2026-04-18

### Fixed
- **Closed both sorrys in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Premise.lean`'s consistency-collapse theorems** (`mustInView_iff_mustInView'_of_consistent`, `canInView_iff_canInView'_of_consistent`). Added five reusable monotonicity helpers that pin the premise algebra to its set-theoretic content: `propIntersection_anti_of_subset` (more premises → smaller intersection), `followsFrom_mono_of_subset` (more premises → more consequences), `isCompatibleWith_anti_of_subset` (fewer premises → easier to be compatible with), `self_mem_consistentSublists` (a consistent list is a sublist of itself), `subset_of_mem_consistentSublists` (every element of `consistentSublists A` is `⊆ A`). Both collapse theorems now witnessed by `f i` itself: when `f i` is consistent, it dominates every consistent sublist `B ⊆ f i`, so the inner `∃ C ⊇ B` is solved by `C := f i` and the inner `∀ C ⊇ B` reduces to a property of subsets of `f i`. Added `import Mathlib.Data.List.Sublists` for `List.mem_sublists`. Build clean through `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Kratzer1977.lean`.

## [0.229.933] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`InquisitiveContent W` is now a mathlib `BoundedDistribLattice` (Stage 5).** Replaces the standalone `LE`/`Top`/`Bot` instances and the eight binary lattice-axiom theorems (`bot_le`, `le_top`, `conj_le_left`, `conj_le_right`, `le_conj`, `left_le_inqDisj`, `right_le_inqDisj`, `inqDisj_le`) with proper mathlib typeclass instances: `PartialOrder` (entailment, `P ≤ Q := P.props ⊆ Q.props`), `Lattice` (binding `⊓ = conj`, `⊔ = inqDisj`), `DistribLattice` (free from `Set.union_inter_distrib_left`-style distributivity on `Set (Set W)`), `OrderTop` (with `⊤ = top`, `top.props = Set.univ`), and `OrderBot` (with `⊥ = bot`, `bot.props = {∅}`). Three convenience equalities (`inf_eq_conj`, `sup_eq_inqDisj`, `top_eq`/`bot_eq`) re-expose the named operations after they're hidden behind lattice notation. Net effect: all of mathlib's lattice/order API now applies — `inf_le_left`, `le_sup_right`, `sup_le`, `inf_sup_left` (distributivity), `bot_le`, `le_top`, plus interval/downset/lattice-hom theorems for free. Smoke-tested against `inf_le_left`, `le_sup_left`, `inf_sup_left`, `bot_le`, `le_top` in `scratch/test_inq_lattice.lean`. Build clean (946 jobs through `Linglib.Core.Mood` and `Phenomena.Complementation.Studies.TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018`). `CompleteLattice` instance (arbitrary `iInf`/`iSup` over families, with the empty-`iSup` = `bot` boundary case) deferred to a follow-up; the binary lattice covers the immediate downstream use cases (verb-meaning composition needs `⊓` for conjunction and `⊔` for disjunction of clausal complements).

## [0.229.932] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Merged `Phenomena/Questions/NandaoQs.lean` + `NandaoQsAnalysis.lean` → `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean`.** Per CLAUDE.md study-file convention (`Studies/AuthorYear.lean`) and the "self-contained study files" guidance: data, fragment-bridge theorems, and theory-bridge theorems for @cite{zheng-2025}'s nandao-Q felicity now live in one author-year file. Namespace `Phenomena.Questions.NandaoQs(.Bridge)` → `Phenomena.Questions.Studies.Zheng2025`. `Linglib.lean` updated.

### Fixed
- **Closed all 4 sorrys in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean`.** Added two private helpers `compatibleWith_iff_exists` (compatible ↔ B_K ∩ ⟦φ⟧ ≠ ∅) and `followsFrom_iff_forall` (followsFrom ↔ B_K ⊆ ⟦φ⟧) bridging the Bool-decided primitives to ∃/∀ over `propIntersection`. With those helpers in hand, structured proofs close `partition_implies_entailment` (case-split on `k.base = ∅`; non-empty case picks `w₀ ∈ B_K`, uses `foldl_refine_preserves_colocated` + `colocated_same_phi` to show all worlds in B_K share φ-value, then dispatches on `φ w₀`), `kernel_duality` (might φ ↔ ¬(must ¬φ) via `push Not`), `must_perhaps_contradiction` (must φ → ¬might ¬φ), and `cant_might_exclusion` (can't φ → ¬might φ).
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/BiasedPQ.lean` `evidentialBias_duality`.** Pre-existing build break after `Kratzer.duality` migrated to `W → Prop`. Coerce `BProp` arguments via `(fun w => p w = true)` and convert `(fun w' => ¬(p w' = true))` to `(fun w' => !p w' = true)` via `Bool.not_eq_true` to match the duality conclusion.

## [0.229.931] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Algebraic structure on `InquisitiveContent` per @cite{puncochar-2019} §2 (Stage 4).** Extends `Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` with the basic inquisitive lattice: `conj P Q` (∧, props = `P.props ∩ Q.props`, the @cite{puncochar-2019} p. 299 ∧-clause), `inqDisj P Q` (⩒, props = `P.props ∪ Q.props`, the inquisitive disjunction clause — distinct from classical ∨, whose support clause involves splitting the state and is deferred), `top` (props = `Set.univ`, the trivial inquiry), `bot` (props = `{∅}`, the unsatisfiable inquiry); `Top` and `Bot` instances, plus the entailment order `LE` instance (`P ≤ Q := P.props ⊆ Q.props` — read as "every state resolving `P` also resolves `Q`"). Lattice-axiom theorems: `bot_le`, `le_top`, `conj_le_left`, `conj_le_right`, `le_conj`, `left_le_inqDisj`, `right_le_inqDisj`, `inqDisj_le`. Two characterization theorems pin the algebra to the type-level constructors: (1) `polar_eq_inqDisj p : polar p = inqDisj (declarative p) (declarative pᶜ)` — grounds the @cite{theiler-etal-2018} Figure 2(a) `polar` constructor in @cite{puncochar-2019}'s `?α := α ⩒ ¬α` (the `¬α` reduction `||¬α|| = declarative pᶜ` for atomic α follows from the support clause of negation reducing to incompatibility on Set W); (2) `isDeclarative_iff_eq_declarative_info P : P.isDeclarative ↔ P = declarative P.info` — the **principal-ideal characterization** from @cite{puncochar-2019} p. 298, made into a Lean theorem rather than a docstring claim. `@[ext]` lemma added so `apply ext + ext q` unpacks structure equality through `Set` extensionality. Classical disjunction `∨`, negation `¬`, and implication `→` deferred (their support clauses involve quantification over information states and need a separate development of the support relation). Build clean (946 jobs through dependent files).

## [0.229.930] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Temporal/Allen.lean` — the canonical 13-relation Allen algebra (@cite{allen-1983}), with full existence + uniqueness proved.** Step 1 of a mathlib-style Tense-API redesign motivated by reviewing @cite{declerck-2006} (Volume 1 of *The Grammar of the English Verb Phrase*, with Susan Reed and Bert Cappelle, Topics in English Linguistics 60-1, Mouton de Gruyter). Adds `Core.Time.AllenRelation` as an inductive with thirteen constructors (`precedes`, `meets`, `overlaps`, `finishedBy`, `contains`, `starts`, `equal`, `startedBy`, `during`, `finishes`, `overlappedBy`, `metBy`, `precededBy`), each with a per-constructor docstring giving its defining inequality. Methods: `inverse` (with `inverse_inverse` and `inverse_eq_self_iff` characterizing `equal` as the unique self-inverse), `all` + `mem_all` (exhaustiveness as a list), `holds : AllenRelation → Interval Time → Interval Time → Prop` (the central predicate), `holds_inverse` (`r.holds i j ↔ r.inverse.holds j i` — the algebraic symmetry of the inverse operation, proved by `cases` + `simp` with `and_comm`/`and_left_comm`/`eq_comm`), `holds_decidable` instance for `[DecidableEq Time] [DecidableRel (· < ·)]`. **Existence and uniqueness both proven** (no sorrys): (a) `holds_exists (i j) : ∃ r, r.holds i j` by trichotomy on `i.finish vs j.start`, refined through `i.start vs j.finish`, `i.start vs j.start`, `i.finish vs j.finish` — explicit witness for each of the 13 leaves; (b) `holds_unique (i j) (hi : i.start < i.finish) (hj : j.start < j.finish) (r₁ r₂) : r₁.holds i j → r₂.holds i j → r₁ = r₂` factored through a structural **signature**: `def signature (i j) : Ordering × Ordering × Ordering × Ordering` records the four pairwise `sgn` (lt/eq/gt) comparisons between `i`'s endpoints and `j`'s, and `def AllenRelation.expectedSig` tabulates the unique 4-tuple forced by each atom's defining inequalities under non-degeneracy. Two 13-case lemmas — `expectedSig_injective` (distinct atoms have distinct signatures, by `cases <;> cases <;> first | rfl | simp`) and `signature_of_holds` (every holds-witness forces its atom's signature, by `cases r <;>` per-component `Prod.ext` + `sgn_lt`/`sgn_eq`/`sgn_gt` + `order`) — combine into a one-line term-level uniqueness proof. This factoring fits in the default heartbeat budget (build ~10s) where the brute-force `cases r₁ <;> cases r₂ <;> order` over 169 cells needed `set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in`. The non-degeneracy hypothesis on `holds_unique` is **essential and intrinsic** to Allen's framework, not a formalization artifact: on a degenerate interval `⟨t, t, le_refl t⟩`, `meets` (`t = t`), `metBy` (`t = t`), and `equal` (`t = t ∧ t = t`) all hold simultaneously — the docstring documents this and notes that @cite{allen-1983} sidesteps it by assuming strict intervals throughout. Predicate bridges connect existing `Interval` predicates to unions of Allen atoms: `precedes_iff_allen` and `meets_iff_allen` are `Iff.rfl`; `isBefore_iff_allen` exhibits `≤` as `{precedes, meets}` (the conflation Allen removes); `isAfter_iff_allen` mirrors with `{precededBy, metBy}`; `subinterval_iff_allen` decomposes containment as `{starts, equal, finishes, during}` via two-way trichotomy on `start ≤ start` and `finish ≤ finish`; `finalSubinterval_iff_allen` further restricts to `{finishes, equal}`. Identity-composition lemmas: `holds_equal_left`/`holds_equal_right` (equal as two-sided identity), `holds_precedes_trans` (chained via `j.valid` since `i.finish < j.start ≤ j.finish < k.start`), `holds_during_trans`/`holds_contains_trans`. The full 13×13 composition table (169 entries, the algebra Allen 1983 derives) is TODO; only the principal transitives used by tense theory are provided. Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `Core.Temporal.Time` and before `Core.Temporal.Reichenbach`. Build clean (865 jobs), no warnings, no sorrys.

## [0.229.929] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018.lean` — forcing study for `InquisitiveContent`.** Stage 3 of the InquisitiveContent line of work (Stages 1–2 = 0.229.925, 0.229.927). Formalizes Section 2 of @cite{theiler-etal-2018} ("A Uniform Semantics for Declarative and Interrogative Complements", *Journal of Semantics* 35(3): 409–466) — the inquisitive-semantics framework for sentence meanings — and exhibits the **mention-some forcing example** from Section 3.1 (building on @cite{george-2011}) that justifies `InquisitiveContent` over `Setoid`. Two parts: (1) **Figure 2 examples** — a 4-world model (Amy left in `0, 1`; didn't leave in `2, 3`) with `amyLeftMeaning := declarative amyLeft` and `didAmyLeaveMeaning := polar amyLeft`; four characteristic theorems pin the paper's (info × inquisitive) classification: declarative is `isInformative` ∧ `¬ isInquisitive`, polar is `¬ isInformative` ∧ `isInquisitive`. (2) **Mention-some forcing** — a 3-world model (`MS = Fin 3`) with overlapping alternatives `msNewstopia = {0, 1}` and `msCellulose = {1, 2}` (world `1` is where both stores sell, hence in both alternatives — exactly the structural feature partitions cannot represent). Theorem `mentionSome_not_partition_derived (r : Setoid MS) : fromSetoid r ≠ mentionSome` proves the mention-some content is **not** in the image of `Setoid → InquisitiveContent`: if it were, both alternatives would lie in equivalence classes containing world `1`, forcing those classes to coincide via `Setoid.eq_of_mem_classes`; then `{0, 2}` would be in `props` but is contained in neither alternative — contradiction. This is the existence theorem for the architectural decision in `Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`'s "Architectural note" (added 0.229.922): `InquisitiveContent` is a **strictly more expressive** representation, so the sibling-structure architecture is empirically necessary, not a redundancy. Scope-limited as per the docstring: §3 (false-answer-sensitive truthful resolutions), §§4–6 (verb-meaning lexicalization, predicates of relevance), and Appendices A–B (comparison with @cite{uegaki-2015}, formal proofs) deferred. Wired into `Linglib.lean`. Build clean.

## [0.229.928] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Case/DependentCaseDerivations.lean` → `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Baker2015.lean`.** The file's contents (cross-linguistic algorithm derivations for German, Turkish, Basque, Hindi, Georgian) match @cite{baker-2015}'s typological survey in *Case: Its Principles and Its Parameters* (CUP) — Baker's monograph takes @cite{marantz-1991}'s dependent case algorithm and runs it across accusative, ergative, and split-ergative columns, which is exactly what this file does. Previously had no single-paper provenance and lived outside `Studies/`; the relocation gives it author-year anchoring parallel to the existing `Marantz1991.lean` (Georgian-focused, abstract-vs-morphological case spell-out) and `BakerVinokurova2010.lean` (Sakha-focused). Docstring rewritten to lead with @cite{baker-2015}'s framework, reference the algorithm's @cite{marantz-1991}/@cite{baker-vinokurova-2010} ancestry, and explicitly defer to the sister study files for content not in Baker's column-sweep. Namespace `Phenomena.Case.DependentCaseDerivations` → `Phenomena.Case.Studies.Baker2015`. `Linglib.lean` import updated. No code or theorem changes. Build clean (297 jobs).

### Added
- **Phase 1 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: `PartialOrder Core.Case` instance + `Caha2009` cross-Fragment study.** Adds Caha's containment hierarchy as the *canonical* order on `Core.Case` via a typeclass instance in `Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/CaseContainment.lean` (§ 13). The order definition is disjunctive — `c₁ ≤ c₂ := c₁ = c₂ ∨ ∃ n m, containmentRank c₁ = some n ∧ containmentRank c₂ = some m ∧ n < m` — which makes off-hierarchy cases (ERG, ABS, INST, COM, …) *incomparable* with on-hierarchy ones rather than forcing them into the order. That incomparability *is* the theoretical content: @cite{caha-2009}'s containment is silent on those cases, and the partial order preserves that silence by construction. Reflexivity comes from the left disjunct; antisymmetry and transitivity reduce to `Nat.lt` properties on the right disjunct, with `Option.some.inj` extracting the rank equalities. Exposes `LE`, `Preorder`, `PartialOrder` instances; sanity theorems `nom_le_acc`, `acc_le_gen`, `gen_le_dat`, `dat_le_loc` confirm the four-step chain, and `erg_incomparable_nom` / `nom_incomparable_erg` confirm off-hierarchy incomparability. Other potential orders on `Case` (Blake's typological frequency, per-language syncretism graphs) live as named `def`s used locally via `letI` rather than as competing instances — the canonical instance goes to the universal claim that state-of-the-art syntax (configurational case + nanosyntactic spellout) assumes. CaseContainment.lean § 14 adds `respectsCahaContainment : List Case → Bool` (decidable contiguous-prefix check using the four hierarchy implications) as the predicate consumers can apply to any inventory. New study file `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Caha2009.lean` instantiates the prediction across 22 Fragment case inventories: 20 conformers (Czech, German, Greek, Hindi, Hungarian, Icelandic, Japanese, Korean, Latin, Mongolian, Polish, Russian, Serbian, Slovenian, SwissGerman, Tamil, Telugu, Turkish, Ukrainian, Yakut) plus 2 *predicted* violators with explanatory theorems — `dargwa_ergative` (ergative alignment puts ABS where NOM would be; ERG/ABS are off-hierarchy) and `finnish_dat_gap` (no dedicated dative; allative -lle covers recipient function per @cite{blake-1994} Ch. 6's ALL → DAT extension). Both violations sit naturally in the partial order's "incomparable" region rather than being counterexamples. Per-Fragment density gives the interconnection-density payoff: any change to a Fragment's inventory or to `containmentRank` breaks exactly one theorem. Naming follows the project's author-year study convention (parallel to `Bobaljik2012.lean`, `BakerVinokurova2010.lean`) — Caha 2009 is *the* paper proposing containment, so the cross-linguistic typological claim derived from it is its study file. Wired into `Linglib.lean`. Build clean (383 jobs). Future phases (not in this commit): Phase 2 (relocate `Phenomena/Case/Compare.lean` to `Studies/`), Phase 3 (reframe `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean` docstring), Phase 4 (`Theories/Syntax/Case/Licensing.lean` for Kalin and Coon-Keine).

## [0.229.927] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean` — faithful `Setoid → InquisitiveContent` embedding.** Stage 2 of the InquisitiveContent line of work (Stage 1 = 0.229.925). `fromSetoid r : InquisitiveContent W` carries `props = {q | q = ∅ ∨ ∃ c ∈ r.classes, q ⊆ c}` — a non-empty information state resolves the issue iff every world in it is `r`-equivalent (i.e., `q` is contained in some equivalence class). The maximal such propositions are exactly the equivalence classes / alternatives. Two characterizing theorems: (1) `info_fromSetoid` — informative content of a partition-derived inquiry is `Set.univ` (every world is in some class), formalizing the standard partition-semantics view that Setoid-based inquiry is **non-informative** (raises an issue, supplies no information); (2) `isInquisitive_fromSetoid_of_two_classes` — if `r` has two `r`-distinct elements, `fromSetoid r` is inquisitive in `InquisitiveContent`'s sense (`info ∉ props`), since `Set.univ` cannot be contained in any single equivalence class. The trivial setoid (one class) yields a declarative — matching the partition-semantics intuition that a one-cell partition raises no real issue. The embedding is faithful but **not surjective** (and we don't claim it is): mention-some, intermediate-exhaustive, and conditional question alternatives are non-disjoint or non-exhaustive and so are not in the image. Mirrors mathlib's `Set.toFinset` / `Filter.principal` / `UniformSpace.toTopologicalSpace` "less general inside more general" pattern. `Core/Mood.lean` re-export hub updated.

## [0.229.926] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Kratzer1977.lean`** — Phenomenon-layer integration test for `Core/IntensionalLogic/Premise.lean`. Formalizes the New Zealand judgments scenario from §1.3 of @cite{kratzer-1977} (= Chapter 1 of @cite{kratzer-2012}), the canonical worked example motivating Kratzer's revision from Defs 5–6 (necessity = consequence; possibility = compatibility) to Defs 7–8 (quantified over consistent sublists). A 4-world `Fin 4` model encodes the two contingent dimensions (`p` = murder is a crime, `q` = deer responsible); the inconsistent premise set `A = [p, q, ¬q]` represents *what the New Zealand judgments provide*. Sentence-by-sentence theorems (`sentence_7_must_p`, `sentence_8_not_must_negP`, `sentence_9_can_q`, `sentence_10_can_negQ`, `sentence_14_not_can_negP`) verify each prediction by `decide`. Two contrast theorems (`def5_vs_def7_negP`, `def6_vs_def8_q`) pin the bug-vs-fix asymmetry: the unrevised `mustInView` trivializes by *ex falso quodlibet* (entails `¬p`), and `canInView` rejects `q` (no world satisfies `A ∪ {q}`) — exactly where the revised `mustInView'` and `canInView'` recover the intuitive predictions. Namespace `Phenomena.Modality.Studies.Kratzer1977`. Wired into `Linglib.lean`. Build clean.

## [0.229.925] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` — bundled `InquisitiveContent W` per @cite{theiler-etal-2018} Definition 1.** Stage 1 of the InquisitiveContent line of work prescribed by the @cite{puncochar-2016}/@cite{puncochar-2019}/@cite{ciardelli-groenendijk-roelofsen-2018} architectural note in `POSWQ.lean` (added 0.229.922). The structure carries `props : Set (Set W)` (the propositions resolving the issue) plus the two defining constraints — `contains_empty` (non-emptiness, normalized as `∅ ∈ props` so it composes with downward closure) and `downward_closed` (any subset of a resolving proposition also resolves). Six predicates/accessors: `alt` (maximal propositions = alternatives, @cite{theiler-etal-2018} Def 2), `info` (informative content as `⋃₀ P.props`, mathlib-native `Set W`), `trueAt`, `isInformative`, `isInquisitive`, `isDeclarative`. Two Figure 2 constructors: `declarative p` (the principal ideal `{q | q ⊆ p}` — single alternative `p`, the meaning of *Amy left*) and `polar p` (alternatives are `p` and `pᶜ` — the meaning of *Did Amy leave?*, non-informative). Six theorems aligned to Figure 2: `info_declarative`, `isDeclarative_declarative`, `not_isInquisitive_declarative`, `info_polar`, `not_isInformative_polar`, and `isInquisitive_polar_iff` (polar question is inquisitive iff the proposition is non-trivial — the trivial cases collapse to declaratives). The algebraic characterization from @cite{puncochar-2019} (declarative propositions = principal ideals in the algebra of information states) is exposed directly as `isDeclarative`. Sibling structure to `Setoid W` per the POSWQ architectural note — does **not** replace `POSWQ.inquiry`; the `Setoid → InquisitiveContent` embedding (Stage 2) is forthcoming in `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean`. `Core/Mood.lean` re-export hub updated; build clean.

## [0.229.924] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Caha containment relocated to the Morphosyntax interface layer.** `Theories/Morphology/{Case,Degree}Containment.lean` → `Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/{Case,Degree}Containment.lean` (new directory, parallel to the existing `Interfaces/{PhonologyMorphology,SyntaxPhonology,SyntaxSemantics,...}/`). The architectural rationale: containment is *exactly* the syntax-morphology interface — the fseq is syntactic but the empirical signature (*ABA in syncretism / suppletion) is morphological, and that's the whole point of @cite{caha-2009}'s Nanosyntax. Previously buried in `Morphology/`, where syntactic case consumers wouldn't naturally look. Namespace `Morphology.{Case,Degree}Containment` → `Interfaces.Morphosyntax.{Case,Degree}Containment` throughout. Generic `Morphology/Containment.lean` (`*ABA` pattern detector on `List Nat` — pure list combinatorics) and the `Morphology/Nanosyntax/` subdirectory (Nanosyntax explicitly rejects the syntax/morphology split, so it stays in Morphology to preserve that theoretical commitment) both **left in place**. 12 consumer files updated: `Linglib.lean` import path; `Theories/Morphology/Nanosyntax/Core.lean` import + docstring backticks; `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean`, `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Bubnov2026.lean`, `Phenomena/Allomorphy/Studies/Aitha2026.lean`, `Phenomena/Comparison/Studies/Bobaljik2012.lean` (qualified-name calls and opens); `Fragments/{German,Tamil,Finnish,Telugu,Latin}/Case.lean`, `Fragments/Latin/Adjectives.lean`, `Fragments/English/Modifiers/Adjectives.lean` (imports + opens). Build clean (2739 jobs across the 14 directly-affected targets). Sets up Phase 1 of the case-API mathlib reorganization: a `PartialOrder Core.Case` instance + cross-Fragment ABA validation will follow as a separate commit on top of this relocation.

Namespace updated to follow the `Phenomena.{Phenomenon}.Studies.{AuthorYear}` convention: `Phenomena.Modality.EpistemicThresholdBridge` → `Phenomena.Modality.Studies.YingEtAl2025`. ## [0.229.922] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Two Punčochář bib entries + architectural-direction note in `Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`.** (1) `puncochar-2016` ("A Generalization of Inquisitive Semantics", *Journal of Philosophical Logic* 45(4): 399–428, doi 10.1007/s10992-015-9379-1) and (2) `puncochar-2019` ("Substructural Inquisitive Logics", *The Review of Symbolic Logic* 12(2): 296–330, doi 10.1017/S1755020319000017) added to `references.bib` with verified DOIs and pages (verified against the actual PDFs). Bibliography regenerated (1563 entries). (3) New "Architectural note: Setoid vs. InquisitiveContent" section in the `POSWQ.lean` file docstring laying out the design choice: we commit `inquiry : Setoid W` (partition-based) and document the algebraic generalization to `InquisitiveContent W` (downward-closed nonempty sets of information states) as the state-of-the-art alternative — citing @cite{puncochar-2016} for the lattice-of-logics characterization, @cite{puncochar-2019} for the substructural information-model frame (declarative propositions = principal ideals), and @cite{ciardelli-groenendijk-roelofsen-2018} for the standard inquisitive-semantics presentation. The note explicitly defers the lift, identifies @cite{theiler-etal-2018} (uniform semantics for declarative + interrogative complements; derives mention-some and intermediate-exhaustive readings that partition theory provably cannot) as the forcing study that would trigger building `InquisitiveContent W`, and prescribes the **sibling-structure** architecture (parallel to Setoid with a faithful embedding) following mathlib's `Set`/`Finset` and `Filter`/`Ultrafilter` precedent rather than collapsing the two.

## [0.229.921] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Kratzer/Lumping.lean`.** First Kratzer-2012-grounded module in the new `Conditionals/Kratzer/` directory. Sits on top of `Core.IntensionalLogic.Situations` (the `SituationFrame` carrier with `[PartialOrder Index]`). Defines the situation-semantics logical relations (`IsTrue`, `IsValid`, `IsConsistent`, `IsCompatible`, `Follows`, `Equiv` — Kratzer 2012 §5.3.3, p. 118) and the lumping relation `Lumps p q w` per the official definition (p. 118): `p w ∧ ∀ s, s ≤ w → p s → q s`. Basic lemmas: `Lumps.true_left`/`true_right`/`local_impl`/`self`/`trans`/`and`/`of_stronger`/`weaken`/`of_universal`. PWS-reduction theorem `lumps_discrete` shows that under the discrete order from `Frame.toDiscreteSituationFrame`, lumping collapses to joint truth (`Lumps p q w ↔ p w ∧ q w`). Counterfactual machinery of §5.4.4 (admissible Base Sets, Crucial Set, would/might) deferred to a forthcoming sibling file.

### Changed
- **`Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Restrictor.lean` polymorphized.** Generalized from hardcoded `World` to `{W : Type*} [DecidableEq W] [Fintype W]`; consequents migrated from `BProp World` (Bool) to `W → Prop`, matching the upstream Prop-based API in `Modality/Kratzer/Operators.lean` (`necessity`/`possibility`/`K_axiom`/`duality`). Antecedents stay `W → Bool` because they are consed onto the modal base (`List (W → Bool)`) by `restrictedBase` — the enumerable factual content stays computable. Removed import of `Theories.Semantics.Attitudes.Intensional`. Two `omit [...] in` clauses added (`kratzer_material_eq_conditional`, `double_restriction`) to silence unused-section-var warnings on theorems that don't need the Fintype/DecidableEq constraints.

## [0.229.920] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Nominal.ArticleInventory` Bool→Prop migration.** The six morphological-presence fields (`hasIndefinite`, `hasUniqueArticle`, `hasAnaphoricArticle`, `uniqueAnaphoricSyncretism`, `hasDemonstrative`, `hasPossessive`) migrated from `Bool` to `Prop` with paired `[Decidable …]` instance fields and an `attribute [instance]` declaration to register them globally — aligns the type with mathlib's Prop-with-Decidable convention for propositional positions (see `feedback_bool_migration_scope.md`). `licensesKind` now returns `Prop`; its decidability is derived structurally via `@instDecidableOr`/`@instDecidableAnd` over the field instances. **Parser-conflict workaround**: `open Core.IntensionalLogic.Variables` (which scopes the `g[n ↦ x]` notation) now appears *after* the structure declaration — when in scope, the `[`-prefixed notation token clashes with structure instance-field syntax `[name : Type]`. Five fragment files (`Fragments/{English,German,Thai,Mandarin,Shan}/Definiteness.lean`) updated `:= true/false` → `:= True/False`. Downstream theorem updates: `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Schwarz2009.lean` (§4 `german_two_articles`/`english_syncretic_articles` use direct Prop conjuncts + `⟨trivial, trivial, id⟩` / `⟨trivial, trivial, trivial⟩`); `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` (`blocking_strategy_correspondence`, the five `licensesKind` predictions for shan/english/german, plus `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct` rewritten with `congrArg ArticleInventory.hasUniqueArticle h` to derive `True = False`). `deriving Repr, DecidableEq` dropped from `ArticleInventory` (Prop fields don't admit them); cascading `deriving` removals on `Phenomena/Anaphora/Typology.lean::PronounSystemDatum` (Repr, BEq) and `Phenomena/Anaphora/DonkeyAnaphora.lean::DonkeyArticleDatum` (Repr).

## [0.229.919] - 2026-04-18

### Fixed
- **Post-relocation wiring fixes for `Fragments/Mongolian/Case.lean` and `Phenomena/Anaphora/Studies/Gong2022.lean`.** Discovered during a clean full-library build after the 0.229.918 bulk commit. Both files used `CaseSystemConfig`/`NPInDomain`/`dependentAccusative` transitively through `LateMerger.lean`, which previously re-exported them via its old `import DependentCase`; the relocation broke that chain. Fix: explicit `import Linglib.Theories.Syntax.Case.Dependent` on Mongolian (which uses `CaseSystemConfig`), and `open Syntax.Case` on both files (alongside the existing `open Minimalism` they need for `LateMerger`/`ChainPosition`).

### Documented (retroactive — bundled into 0.229.918)
- **`CaseVal` (Minimalism's 7-value enum) eliminated; unified onto `Core.Case` (Blake's 19-value typological inventory).** Both types contained the same NOM/ACC/GEN/DAT/ERG/ABS constructors, so the Minimalism layer's redundant `CaseVal` was a separate-stipulation-then-bridge anti-pattern. `Core.Case` is now the single canonical case type across the library. Touched: `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/{Features,CaseFilter,Modification}.lean` (`FeatureVal.case : CaseVal → ...` → `Core.Case → ...`; `DPFeatures.withCase`; `MAGFeatureType.kappa`); `Fragments/Mayan/{Chol,Qanjobal,Kaqchikel}/Agreement.lean` (Kaqchikel also drops `.toCase` indirection now that the types coincide); `Phenomena/Case/Studies/{Marantz1991,Aissen2003,Woolford1997}.lean` (`getCase!`/`georgianSpellout` retypings; `WCase.toCaseVal` → `WCase.toCoreCase`); `Phenomena/Case/{DependentCaseDerivations,Compare}.lean` (`CaseVal.{erg,abs,nom}.toCase` → `Core.Case.{erg,abs,nom}`; `np.case.toCase` → `np.case`); `Phenomena/Ergativity/Studies/{CoonMateoPedroPreminger2014,Imanishi2020}.lean` (`AbstractCase.toCaseVal` → `AbstractCase.toCoreCase`; `caseVal_bridge_*` → `coreCase_bridge_*`).
- **`DependentCase.lean` relocated out of `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/` into `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`.** Dependent case (Marantz/Baker) is a *competing framework* to Agree-based case, not a Minimalist component; the file location now reflects the theoretical commitment. Namespace `Minimalism` → `Syntax.Case`. Import in `Linglib.lean` updated; importers (`Phenomena/Case/Studies/{BakerVinokurova2010,Marantz1991,Aissen2003,Woolford1997,Ozaki2026}.lean`, `Phenomena/Case/{Compare,DependentCaseDerivations}.lean`, `Fragments/Yakut/Case.lean`) swapped to `import Linglib.Theories.Syntax.Case.Dependent` + `open Syntax.Case` (added alongside `open Minimalism` for files that also use Voice/CaseFilter). `LateMerger.lean`'s decorative `import DependentCase` replaced with the actually-needed `import ...Minimalism.Core.Basic`.
