## [0.229.917] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood three-lattice unification — `+`, `⋆`, `?` are `inf` in three parallel mathlib lattices.** The deep mathematical observation: each of the three POSW(Q) components (`cs : W → Prop`, `le : W → W → Prop`, `inquiry : Setoid W`) carries an already-mathlib'd lattice, and each of the three updates is meet in its component's lattice. None of this appears in @cite{portner-2018} (which writes (2a) as set intersection and (2b) via Condoravdi-Lauer composition); it is a linglib extension that puts the algebraic story on a uniform footing. (1) **`plus_cs_eq_inf`** (`Core/Mood/POSW.lean` §3, definitional `@[simp]`): `(c.plus p).cs = c.cs ⊓ p` — `+`-update is meet in `W → Prop` (instance chain `Prop.instDistribLattice` → `Pi.instLattice`). (2) **`star_le_eq_inf`** (POSW §6, definitional `@[simp]`): `(c.star p).le = c.le ⊓ promote p` — `⋆`-update is meet in `W → W → Prop`. (3) **`inquire_inquiry_eq_inf`** (POSWQ §2, definitional `@[simp]`, parallel-named): `(c.inquire q).inquiry = c.inquiry ⊓ q` — `?`-update is meet in `Setoid.completeLattice W`, which gives `iInf` over arbitrary index sets for free (so "asking the conjunction of a family of questions" reduces to a one-line `iInf` consequence). (4) **`star_star_comm_le` and `star_star_self_le` refactored from `Iff` (pointwise, with explicit `simp only` + term proof) to full `Eq` (relation level, one-liner via `inf_right_comm` and `inf_assoc + inf_idem`).** The new statements are strictly stronger (whole relation equality instead of pointwise iff) and the proofs are kernel-trivial — both reductions are exactly the lattice laws on `Pi.instLattice` over `Prop`. No downstream consumers (these were added in 0.229.916). (5) **§6 docstring rewritten** to frame `⋆`-update as the relation-side analogue of `+`-update and to credit `Pi.instLattice` for inheritance of `inf_comm`/`inf_assoc`/`inf_idem`. (6) **§8 NormalModality docstring updated**: the K-axiom for `boxCs`/`boxLe` is now described as the universal-quantification-preserves-inf shape over `Pi.instLattice` on `Prop` — the same lattice underlying `+` and `⋆`. (7) **POSW + POSWQ file docstrings** include the three-lattice table inline so the unification is discoverable from the top of each file. Imports added: `Mathlib.Order.PropInstances`, `Mathlib.Order.Lattice` to POSW.lean. Build clean (1119 jobs across POSW + POSWQ + POSWTarget + VerbalMood + Scoreboard + Grano2024).

## [0.229.916] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood mathlib polish — strongest mathematical foundation across `Core/Mood/` + `Theories/Semantics/Mood/`.** (1) **`POSW.lt` → `POSW.le` rename** throughout `Core/Mood/POSW.lean`, `Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`, `Core/Mood/POSWTarget.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`, and `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/Grano2024.lean` (docstrings only) — aligns with mathlib's `Preorder` / `≤` convention; old `boxLt` exposed as `@[deprecated] abbrev` for one cycle. (2) **Subtype `Preorder` instance** on `{w // c.cs w}` derived from POSW reflexivity/transitivity — gives ordered-set machinery (Antitone/Monotone/`≤`-rewriting) for free on the in-CS subtype, replacing per-call destructuring. (3) **`promote` preorder + star algebra**: `promote p w v := p v → p w` is the preorder content of the `⋆`-update, and `star_le_eq` exposes `(c.star p).le w v ↔ c.le w v ∧ promote p w v` as a `@[simp]` lemma. Falling-out theorems `star_star_comm_le` and `star_star_self_le` follow as one-line term proofs from this characterization (commutativity and idempotence of the directive update at the order layer). (4) **Farkas fixed-point**: `le_plus_iff_boxCs : c ≤ c.plus p ↔ c.boxCs p` — the `+`-update is a no-op (under POSW's preorder) iff the proposition is already CS-entailed. The pointwise type-level shadow of @cite{farkas-2003} eq. 11. (5) **`NormalModality` typeclass** (`Core/Mood/POSW.lean`) packages necessitation + K-axiom; instances for `boxCs` and `boxLe` (both inherit normality from the underlying universal quantifier over a domain). `boxAns` is *not* a normal modality — its K-axiom fails when the antecedent is sometimes-false on a partition cell (concrete counterexample: W = {a,b,c}, inquiry {{a,b},{c}}, p false on {a,b}, q non-constant). Documented in the `boxAns` docstring; `boxAns` retains its boolean-closure properties separately. (6) **Indicative/Subjunctive Principles as biconditionals**: `gram_mood_target_informational_iff_indicative` and `gram_mood_target_preferential_iff_subjunctive` (POSWTarget.lean) plus the three `verbal_mood_target_*_iff_*` analogues (VerbalMood.lean) state the strongest type-level form of @cite{portner-2018}'s principles available without a syntax-side mood-features infrastructure — `GramMood`/`VerbalMoodOp` are in bijection with the POSW components they target, so the conditionals collapse to biconditionals. The new §3 docstring in POSWTarget.lean situates this against @cite{farkas-2003}'s update-type alternative. `gram_mood_target_ne_partition` records the complementary closure: verbal mood never targets the partition component (the IllocutionaryMood/.interrogative path). (7) **POSWQ §7→§8 header fix** (the duplicate "Distinctness Witness" was previously the second §7). (8) **Footnote-3 docstring caveat** added to `POSW.plus`: noting @cite{portner-2018} Ch. 4 fn. 3's observation that `c + p` is technically not a POSW (its `le` is not restricted to the new `cs`) — kept as a documentation point rather than a structural change since the existing `le_refl`/`le_trans` proofs only quantify over CS members. Build clean (5139 jobs).
- **`POSW`/`POSWQ` `Preorder` instances anonymized**: dropped the explicit `instance instPreorder` / `instance instPreorderSubtype` names — Lean autogenerates a unique name and these instances are only ever resolved by typeclass inference. Cleaner mathlib-style declaration.

## [0.229.915] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Bundled Pesetsky-followups (Plans A + B).** (A) `Theories/Morphology/CaseContainment.lean` — all 21 `native_decide` invocations replaced with `decide` per the project's mathlib-style preference for kernel-checkable proofs over native compilation; build times unaffected (~9.2s). (B) **`CaseVal.obl` constructor removed from `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/Features.lean`.** The constructor existed only as a Minimalism-internal placeholder and forced an `obl → dat` kludge in `CaseVal.toCase` (the @cite{blake-1994} typology has no separate oblique slot). The four prior consumers — two placeholder probes in `CaseFilter.lean` (`dpNeedsCase`, `DPFeatures.withUnvaluedCase`), one `.kappa .obl` agreement entry in `Fragments/Russian/AdjAgreement.lean`, one sentinel in `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Marantz1991.lean`'s `getCase!` helper, and a sentinel-comparison in `Phenomena/Case/Compare.lean` — were all migrated: the placeholders use `.dat`, the agreement entry was dropped (it was sloppy anyway — `.abl`/`.obl` aren't Russian cases), and `dependent_case_always_assigns` was rewritten to check `.length` (a more meaningful property than the sentinel sniff). `CaseVal.toCase` is now a clean injection with no kludge, plus a new `CaseVal.toCase_injective` theorem.

### Architecture
- **Researched 2024–26 reception of @cite{pesetsky-2013}** before deciding on the placement of `POSCat`. Verdict (cited but not central; dependent-case dominant; no recent handbook centers it; treated as one alternative among several): **POSCat (the four POS=case primitives N/D/V/P) is paper-specific, kept local to `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean`**, not promoted to a shared `Core` or `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/` module. Centralizing Pesetsky's identification would impose a controversial minority commitment on every downstream case formalization.
- **`Fragments/Russian/Case.lean` reverted to theory-neutral form** — just the 6-case inventory + Blake validity. The Pesetsky-specific `pesetskyCore`, `pesetskyCore_subset_inventory`, and `inst_loc_outside_pesetsky` theorems migrated to `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean` (renamed `pesetskyCore_subset_russian_inventory` to keep the cross-file relationship visible). `POSCase` renamed to `POSCat` throughout. Fragments are descriptive infrastructure; theory-specific reductions belong in study files.

## [0.229.914] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Definiteness API Stage 10 — `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/CoppockBeaver2015.lean` and `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Hanink2021.lean` as integration tests for the `Core/Nominal/` substrate.** With Stages 1–9 in place (Frame substrate + `NominalKind` sum + `Maximality` operators + `interpret` + `ArticleInventory`), the two foundational papers behind that substrate now have explicit study files exercising the relevant predicates end-to-end. (1) **`CoppockBeaver2015.lean`** builds a tiny three-entity frame (sun / moon / mars / Unit index) with three restrictors — `theSun` (singleton), `planet` (multi-witness), `kingOfFrance` (empty) — and proves the @cite{coppock-beaver-2015} factorization claims as kernel theorems: `kingOfFrance_uniqueness_without_existence` (Uniqueness vacuous, Existence false), `planet_existence_without_uniqueness`, `theSun_existence_and_uniqueness`. The Russellian collapse (`existsUnique = Existence ∧ Uniqueness`) is then read off as `theSun_existsUnique`, `kingOfFrance_not_existsUnique`, `planet_not_existsUnique`, with each failure attributed to the *specific* missing conjunct. The interpretation alignment theorems (`interpret_theSun = some sun`, `interpret_kingOfFrance = none`, `interpret_planet = none`) confirm that `Core.Nominal.interpret` on `.unique` collapses both presupposition failures to `none` while the §2 diagnostics retain the structural distinction. The negation-projection contrast (`uniqueness_projects_through_negation` vs. `existence_does_not_project_through_negation`) is the empirical payoff. (2) **`Hanink2021.lean`** builds a two-entity, two-index frame (`tableKitchen`/`tableLiving` × `kitchen`/`living`) with a restrictor `tableAtSit0` that consults `interpSitPronoun 0 gs` to fetch the resource situation. The headline theorem `same_description_different_referents` shows that the *same* `.unique tableAtSit0 0` description picks out `tableKitchen` under `gsKitchen` and `tableLiving` under `gsLiving` — operationalizing @cite{hanink-2021}'s claim that the resource situation is a bound variable in the structure, not a free contextual parameter. The orthogonality of entity-binding and situation-binding is verified via `situation_binders_classified` (only `.unique` and `.demonstrative` flag `usesSituationPronoun`) and `anaphoric_independent_of_situation` (anaphoric reads from `g`, not `gs` — the Schwarz weak/strong split mirrored at the substrate). `unique_index_does_not_alter_referent_directly` records that the index argument to `.unique` is structural bookkeeping (which pronoun is bound), not a selector — the restrictor's call to `interpSitPronoun sIdx` does the work. (3) **bib entries**: `coppock-beaver-2015` (Linguistics and Philosophy 38(5):377–435, doi 10.1007/s10988-015-9178-8) and `hanink-2021` (Natural Language & Linguistic Theory 39(2):505–554, doi 10.1007/s11049-020-09483-x) added to `references.bib` — both keys were already used in `Core/Nominal/{Description,Maximality,Interpret}.lean` docstrings. Bibliography regenerated. Build clean (364 jobs each).

## [0.229.913] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Definiteness API Stage 9 — collapse the redundant `markingParams`/`strategy`/`articleType` shim layer in `Fragments.Shan.Definiteness`.** With `Core.Nominal.ArticleInventory` as the single upstream source of truth (see Stages 6–8), Shan's `markingParams : DefMarkingParams` (boolean triple), `strategy : deriveStrategy markingParams = .unmarked`, `articleType : strategyToArticleType (...) = .none_`, and `articleInventory_params_agreement : articleInventory.toMarkingParams = markingParams` were all `rfl`-projections of the same underlying `articleInventory`. They are now removed; `articleInventory.toMarkingParams` and `articleInventory.toMarkingStrategy` are the canonical accesses, and `articleInventory_marking : articleInventory.toMarkingStrategy = .unmarked` is the kept summary theorem. Shan was the only fragment with the redundant shim layer (English/German/Mandarin/Thai are already grounded solely on `articleInventory`). **`Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` §7** is correspondingly updated: `englishParams`/`germanParams`/`thaiParams`/`shanParams` are now defined as `Fragments.{Lang}.Definiteness.articleInventory.toMarkingParams` projections rather than stipulated triples — the fragment-level inventories drive the Moroney typology directly. The old `inventory_agrees_with_params` theorem (now `rfl`-tautological since the params are the inventory projections) is deleted; `derive_all_languages`, `derive_consistent_with_stipulated`, and `blocking_strategy_correspondence` all continue to close by `rfl` since they only inspect the boolean fields. Build clean (988 jobs across 8 root targets).

## [0.229.912] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry.lean` — WALS F101A bridge.** Bridges @cite{dryer-2013-wals}'s "Expression of Pronominal Subjects" classification (711 languages) into the registry's `ProDropProfile` view. (1) `F101A.toAllowsProDrop : ExpressionOfPronominalSubjects → Option Bool` — the standard typological reading: `obligatoryPronounsInSubjectPosition → some false`, four affix/clitic/optional/displaced strategies → `some true`, `mixed → none`. WALS does not record overt PRO, so the bridge is the `allowsProDrop`-only projection — `hasOvertPRO` cannot be derived from F101A alone. (2) `F101A.allowsProDropOf (walsCode : String) : Option Bool` looks up F101A.allData by WALS code and projects via `toAllowsProDrop`. (3) `walsCodeOf : Language → Option String` records F101A coverage of registry languages — English (`"eng"`) and Gã (`"ga"`) are in WALS; SMPM and Büli are not. (4) **Coverage theorems**: `english_agrees_with_F101A` (WALS `obligatoryPronounsInSubjectPosition` ⇒ `allowsProDrop = false`, matches Ostrove's English profile); **`ga_disagrees_with_F101A`** (WALS classifies the Gã subject proclitic as `subjectAffixesOnVerb` → pro-drop under the standard reading, while @cite{allotey-2021} treats it as an overt pronominal clitic that fills subject position → non-pro-drop, overt PRO — the disagreement is surfaced as a named theorem rather than papered over); `walsCovered_languages` (the F101A-covered subset is exactly `{english, ga}`). The Gã case is the kind of substantive theory-vs-typological-data tension the project aims to make visible.

### Changed
- **Typology constructors renamed to mathlib camelCase** (`nullPRO_noProDrop` → `nullPRONoProDrop`, `nullPRO_proDrop` → `nullPROProDrop`, `overtPRO_noProDrop` → `overtPRONoProDrop`, `overtPRO_proDrop` → `overtPROProDrop`). Three references updated in `Ostrove2026.lean` (`smpm_classified`, `english_classified`, `buli_classified`); five references updated in `ProDropRegistry.lean` (`forbidden_cell_empty`, `cellCount_overtPROProDrop_eq_zero`, plus three docstring mentions). Consistent with the renaming done in `Core/NullSubject/Basic.lean` in 0.229.911.

### Fixed
- **Misplaced WALS bridge file deleted.** `Linglib/Core/NullSubject/WALS.lean` (created during the F101A integration plan) was placed in `Core/`, which inverts the dependency hierarchy: WALS-to-typology bridges live in `Phenomena/`, alongside the existing pattern in `Phenomena/Pronouns/Typology.lean` (which imports F39A-F48A). The bridge is now in `Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry.lean`, where the per-language registry profiles already live.

## [0.229.911] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood-arc cleanup PR (Stage 9, follow-up to PR J).** (1) Citation hallucinations corrected in `POSW.lean`, `POSWQ.lean`, `VerbalMood.lean`, `POSWTarget.lean`: removed unverified equation references, attributed substrate components to `@cite{farkas-2003}`, `@cite{condoravdi-lauer-2012}`, `@cite{stalnaker-1978}`. (2) `POSWQ ↔ POSWTarget` overlap resolved via cross-link in module docstrings plus a typed projection `POSWTarget.boxOn : POSWTarget → POSWQ W → (W → Prop) → Prop` that delegates to `boxCs`/`boxLt`/`boxAns` — the typeclass is now operationally meaningful, not just a label. `mood_alignment_decl_ind` renamed to `decl_ind_target_match` with a `@[deprecated]` alias; section docstring rewritten to clarify the Indicative Principle is a one-way conditional, not a target-equality. (3) `QuestionEmbedder` enum (`.factive | .nonfactive`) added in `VerbalMood.lean` with `toVerbalMood := fun _ => .interrogative`, closing the `.interrogative`-unreachable gap left by `MoodSelector` and pair-covering the verbal-mood operator space. (4) Closure theorems `boxAns_or` and `boxAns_imp` added to POSWQ. (5) `polarSetoid` moved from `Scoreboard.lean` (where it was a local definition) to `POSWQ.lean` (where it belongs as the inquiry-component constructor); added `polarSetoid_r`/`polarSetoid_top` simp lemmas; generalized via `Setoid.ofProp`. (6) Wired one downstream consumer: `Grano2024.lean` now exercises `want_verbalMood`/`hope_verbalMood`/`want_target` via `native_decide` at a complement-selection site. New `interp_eq_target_boxOn` on `VerbalMood` proves verbal-mood interpretation factors through `POSWTarget.boxOn`. New §7 in POSWQ adds `POSWQ.plus`/`POSWQ.star` lifted operations with pairwise commutativity proofs.

### Fixed
- Completed concurrent session's `Assignment` alias migration in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` (the `abbrev Assignment (F : Frame) := Core.Assignment F.Entity` wrapper was removed upstream, leaving 11 downstream call sites with `Unknown identifier Assignment` errors). Migrated all `Assignment F` usages to `Core.Assignment F.Entity` in `Composition/Tree.lean`, `Composition/Effects.lean`, `Composition/QuantifierComposition.lean`, `Composition/Glue.lean`, `Interfaces/SyntaxSemantics/VoiceSemantics.lean`, `Interfaces/SyntaxSemantics/Minimalism/Interface.lean`, `Reference/Binding.lean` (also `Assignment.update` → `Core.Assignment.update`), `Anaphora/Studies/Charlow2018.lean`, `Focus/Studies/Rooth1992.lean`, `Generics/Studies/Guerrini2026.lean`, `Syntax/Minimalism/RelativeClauses.lean`. Build clean.

## [0.229.910] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean` — formalization of the POS-as-case thesis from @cite{pesetsky-2013}.** Pesetsky's central reduction (NOM=D, ACC=V, GEN=N, OBL=P) modeled as a four-element `POSCase` enum with a total bridge `POSCase.toCase : POSCase → Core.Case` (image is exactly the Russian core four NOM/ACC/GEN/DAT). (1) **The FA rule** (Feature Assignment, Ch. 8 final form) modeled as `assignDown` — a mutual recursion over `PesTree` (leaves carry case stacks, internal nodes carry a POS label) that prepends the assigner's category to every dominated leaf's stack. (2) **The One-Suffix Rule** (Ch. 3) is a one-line filter `oneSuffix : List POSCase → Option POSCase` that retains only the outermost stratum; Lardil (@cite{richards-2013}) is the same grammar without the filter (`lardilSpellOut := id`). The cross-linguistic divergence is a kernel theorem: `lardilSpellOut (a :: b :: rest) ≠ (oneSuffix _).toList`. (3) **Every noun is "born genitive."** Modeled as `bornGenitive w := .leaf w [.N]`. The Russian/Lardil contrast is shown by two parallel theorems on a single derivation: `oneSuffix (assignDown .V (bornGenitive w)).stack = some .V` (Russian masks the inner N) vs. `lardilSpellOut (assignDown .V (bornGenitive w)).stack = [.V, .N]` (Lardil shows both). (4) **Russian paucals (Ch. 4–6).** `RusNumClass` (paucal/higher/quant) with lexical entries for *dva*, *tri*, *četyre*, *pjat'*, *šest'*, *mnogo*, *skol'ko*. Pesetsky's unifying claim — *all three classes assign N (genitive) to their complement noun* — is the kernel theorem `numerals_all_assign_N`, with per-numeral surface-case derivations (`dva_stola_surface_gen`, `pjat'_stolov_surface_gen`, `mnogo_studentov_surface_gen`) all yielding `some .N` under One-Suffix. The double-N stack (`paucal_stack_double_N : (applyNumeral dva "stol").stack = [.N, .N]`) makes explicit that paucal-induced GEN is *additional* to the universal born-genitive N. (5) **Subject/object derivations** (`subject_surface_nom`, `object_surface_acc`) instantiate the POS=case thesis end-to-end: bare noun → outer assigner (D for subject, V for object) → One-Suffix yields surface NOM/ACC. Inner stacks are identical (`subject_object_same_inner`). (6) **Bridges** (§7): `pos_image_in_core` proves the four POSCase values map exactly to gen/nom/acc/dat in `Core.Case`; `caha_containment_inverts_pesetsky` connects to @cite{caha-2009}'s containment hierarchy in `Theories/Morphology/CaseContainment.lean` (Caha's morphological containment rank ascends NOM→ACC→GEN; Pesetsky's derivational stack starts with N=GEN at the bottom — the two views are consistent under the inversion); `vrač_morphological_class_unchanged` ties Pesetsky's feminizing-Ж analysis (Ch. 5) into the existing hybrid noun in `Fragments/Russian/Gender.lean` (Class I assignment preserved). (7) **Out-of-scope items documented in the docstring**: full VACC restriction with referent-feature interaction, D-headed PP for POBL↔VACC spatial alternations (Ch. 7), Spell-Out / phase locality on adnominals (Ch. 8). (8) **`Fragments/Russian/Case.lean` enriched** with `pesetskyCore : List Core.Case := [POSCase.D, POSCase.V, POSCase.N, POSCase.P].map POSCase.toCase`, plus `pesetskyCore_subset_inventory` (the four POS-derived cases are all in the Russian inventory) and `inst_loc_outside_pesetsky` (INST and LOC are typological elaborations not in Pesetsky's POS=case mapping). (9) **`references.bib` adds `@richards-2013`** (Lardil "Case Stacking" and the Timing of Case Assignment, Syntax 16(1)) — DOI omitted pending verification. Build clean (890 jobs).

## [0.229.909] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage 8 of the Definiteness API redesign — collapse the named-wrapper layer; canonical `russellIotaList` + `presupOfReferent` become the single source of truth.** The denotational layer for definite descriptions is now expressed by composing two canonical pieces: (a) `Core.Nominal.russellIotaList : List E → (E → Bool) → Option E` — the per-context Russellian iota over a `List E` filtered by a `Bool` predicate (computable counterpart of `Core.Nominal.russellIota`), with `russellIotaList_eq_some_iff` characterizing definedness as filter-equals-singleton; (b) `Core.Presupposition.PrProp.presupOfReferent : (W → Option E) → (E → W → Bool) → PrProp W` — the presupposition+assertion combinator that lifts a partial referent selector and a scope predicate to a `PrProp W`, with `@[simp] presupOfReferent_presup` plus `presupOfReferent_assertion_some/_none` characterizing the projections. (1) **Named wrappers deleted from `Theories/Semantics/Definiteness/Basic.lean`.** `evalDefinite`, `the_uniq`, `the_uniq_w`, `the_fam`, `the_anaphoric`, and the auxiliary equivalence theorems (`the_anaphoric_vacuous_eq_the_uniq`, `familiarity_weaker_existence`, `the_anaphoric_more_restrictive`, `domain_restriction_eq_filter`, `the_uniq_eq_definitePrProp`, `the_uniq_presup_iff_iota`) are gone — they were lossy projections of the same underlying composition. The file retains only the abstract glue (`DiscourseContext`, `definite_presup_iff_iota`, `the_is_every_on_singletons`, `qforceToPresupType`, `qforceToDefiniteness`, `definite_is_uniqueness`, `modifierNecessary`). (2) **`Core/Definiteness.lean` §6 deleted** — the `DefiniteDesc` record (with `unique`/`anaphoric` smart constructors and the trivial equality theorem) was a pure repackaging of `russellIotaList` and added no information; consumers compose the canonical pieces directly. Subsequent sections renumbered. (3) **`Theories/Semantics/Reference/Donnellan.lean` factored.** `definitePrProp` is now defined as `presupOfReferent (attributiveContent domain restrictor) predicate`, with the new `definitePrProp_eq_presupOfReferent` and `@[simp] definitePrProp_presup` making the factoring explicit. The Donnellan attributive use is the same canonical machine with a different selector argument. (4) **Six consumers migrated in one wave.** `Fragments/Akan/Determiners.lean` (`noSem` rewritten), `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` (`bareDefinite`/`demDenotation` return `Option E`; new behavior-based `dem_refines_bare`; `liftToPrProp` helper), `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Elbourne2013.lean` (`the_sit`/`the_sit'` factor through `presupOfReferent`; new `the_sit'_eq_definitePrProp` rfl-bridge to Donnellan; `donkey_uniqueness_from_minimality` and `the_sit_assertion_implies_presup` ported), `Phenomena/SyntacticAmbiguity/Studies/PaapeVasishth2026.lean` (file-local `private def thePresup` replaces 17 `the_uniq` references; simp-arg lists updated to the new lemma names), `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` (`type_shift_referent_agreement` replaces the `_denotation_agreement` form; §12 `demonstrative_adds_spatial_info` rewritten as a behavior equation against `russellIotaList` — the old `presupFilter`-field theorem expressed a record-projection that no longer exists), `Theories/Semantics/Noun/Kind/Dayal2004.lean` (docstring updated). (5) **No bridge-theorem layer.** This refactor follows the "what would mathlib do" principle: there is no thin wrapper retained for ergonomics, and there are no per-paper `the_uniq_eq_X` theorems — wherever a paper needs a per-file convenience name (Elbourne's `the_sit'`, Paape & Vasishth's `thePresup`), that name is defined locally as a direct application of `presupOfReferent`. The `Core.Definiteness` typology vocabulary (`DefPresupType`, `DefMarkingStrategy`, etc.) is unchanged. Build clean for the full Definiteness chain (1004 jobs across 8 root targets).

## [0.229.908] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/NullSubject.lean` → `Core/NullSubject/` subdirectory (Stage A of the 2026-aware refactor).** The single-file `Core/NullSubject.lean` is replaced by a subdirectory of three files, with no top-level `Core/NullSubject.lean` remnant (mathlib idiom, matching `Mathlib/Order/`). (1) **`Core/NullSubject/Basic.lean`** is the moved old content — `ProDropProfile`, `Satisfies` (Prop + Decidable), `null_pro_satisfies`, `overt_no_prodrop_satisfies`, `prodrop_excludes_overt_pro`, `Typology`, `Typology.isAttested`, `classify`, `satisfies_iff_attested` — preserved verbatim so existing consumers (`Ostrove2026.lean`, `Allotey2021.lean`, `ProDropRegistry.lean`) keep working. (2) **`Core/NullSubject/Defs.lean` (new) — subject-context vocabulary.** Four orthogonal axes that suffice for the empirical cases 2026-era pro-drop literature engages with: `Person` (aliased to `UD.Person` for cross-linguistic compatibility — partial pro-drop in Hebrew/BP/Finnish requires per-person predicates), `Finiteness` (finite/nonfinite — distinguishes pro-drop loci from PRO loci), `ClauseRole` (matrix/embeddedFinite/controlSubject — distinguishes matrix pro-drop from embedded-pro-drop asymmetries from PRO position), `ABarStatus` (insitu/aBarExtracted — anti-agreement effect in Tarifit/Fiorentino/Wolof, @cite{baier-2018}). `SubjectContext` is the structure of the four-way cross-classification; `Exponent` is `null | overt`. Convenience constructors `SubjectContext.matrixFinite`/`embeddedFinite`/`controlled`/`matrixExtracted` cover the canonical 3rd-person in-situ loci that study files reach for. (3) **`Core/NullSubject/Universals.lean` (new) — abstract universal interface.** `SubjectAssignment := SubjectContext → Exponent` is the abstract interface a syntactic theory must provide to be evaluated against the universals. `SubjectAssignment.hasOvertPRO`/`allowsProDrop` are 3rd-person-canonical Boolean projections; `SubjectAssignment.Satisfies` (Prop + Decidable) is @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal stated abstractly. `toProDropProfile : SubjectAssignment → ProDropProfile` bridges to `Basic.lean`'s legacy two-Boolean view; `toProDropProfile_satisfies_iff` proves the bridge is information-preserving for the universal (`Iff.rfl`). (4) **`Linglib.lean`** adds the two new imports next to the existing (auto-resolved) `Linglib.Core.NullSubject.Basic` line. Consumer imports in `Ostrove2026.lean` / `Allotey2021.lean` resolve to `.Basic` (auto-resolved during the move; no semantic change). Build clean (1020 jobs). The Minimalism-side bridge (`MinPronInventory.subjectAssignment` projection + `subjectAssignment_overtPRO_iff` theorem) and per-language assignments (Italian, BP, Finnish, Hebrew, Tarifit) remain for follow-up stages — Stage A is the framework-side scaffolding.

## [0.229.907] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry.lean` — unified endpoint for the pro-drop / overt-PRO universal.** Coverage registry for every language formalized against @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal. (1) **`Language` enum** — inductive type `english | smpm | buli | ga` (with `DecidableEq`, `Repr`, `Fintype` derived) replacing the brittle "list of (String × Profile) pairs" pattern: the index type *is* the registry. Adding a constructor here is the only registration step. (2) **`profile : Language → ProDropProfile`** — the unified endpoint, sourcing each language's profile from its study file (`Ostrove2026.englishProfile`, `Ostrove2026.smpmProfile`, `Ostrove2026.buliProfile`, `Allotey2021.gaProfile`). (3) **Two universal theorems closed by `cases L <;> decide`.** `all_satisfy : ∀ L, (profile L).Satisfies` is the universal asserted concretely over the cross-linguistic database, kernel-checked by case enumeration. `forbidden_cell_empty : ∀ L, (profile L).classify ≠ Typology.overtPRO_proDrop` is the falsifiable empty-cell claim — the explicit form of "the typology has a forbidden cell." Adding a future language whose profile lands in `overtPRO_proDrop` breaks both proofs at typecheck. (4) **`cellCount`** via `Finset.univ.filter` enables histogram queries over the registry; `cellCount_overtPRO_proDrop_eq_zero` is the cardinality form of `forbidden_cell_empty`. (5) **Genre placement.** Lives at `Phenomena/Pronouns/` (subject pronoun realization is the relevant typological domain) as a sibling to `Pronouns/Typology.lean` (which stays a pure WALS-data importer per the established `Typology.lean` genre). The registry imports Phenomena/Studies and is therefore not a "data" file in the CLAUDE.md sense — it is a survey-level synthesis. **Mathlib idioms employed (per design discussion):** indexable type instead of `String` keys; `Fintype` + `Finset.univ` instead of manually-maintained list; universal as `∀ L : Language, ...` theorem decidable by case enumeration. Imports added to `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.229.906] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Language inventories moved out of Core into language Fragments (architectural fix).** Stage 6 (0.229.901) and Stage 7 (0.229.905) had stipulated `ArticleInventory.{english,german,mandarin,thai,shan}` and their per-language `_marking` consistency theorems inside `Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean`, breaking the Theory→Fragment dependency hierarchy: language data lives in `Fragments/`, not in `Core/`. (1) **Four new fragment files** mirror the existing `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` skeleton — `Fragments/English/Definiteness.lean`, `Fragments/German/Definiteness.lean`, `Fragments/Mandarin/Definiteness.lean`, `Fragments/Thai/Definiteness.lean` — each defines `articleInventory : Core.Nominal.ArticleInventory` and `articleInventory_marking : articleInventory.toMarkingStrategy = ...` proving the language's Moroney cell by `rfl`. (2) **`Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` extended** with `articleInventory`, `articleInventory_marking`, and `articleInventory_params_agreement` (proves `articleInventory.toMarkingParams = markingParams` by `rfl`, witnessing that the inventory is the upstream object from which the existing `markingParams` is the boolean projection — both representations live side-by-side in the Shan fragment for now). (3) **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` stripped** to just the `ArticleInventory` structure, `licensesKind`, `toMarkingParams`, `toMarkingStrategy`, `toArticleType`, the universal `licensesKind_bare` simp lemma, and the universal `toArticleType_eq_strategyToArticleType`. The five language defs and five `_marking` theorems are gone. (4) **Cross-language collapse theorems migrated to `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` §14** (`english_mandarin_articleType_collapse`, `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct`) — these are observations *about* a typological pattern, not facts about Core, so they belong at the Phenomena layer. (5) **`Moroney2021.lean` §14 rewritten** to import the four new fragments (plus the existing Shan one), introduce `englishInv`/`germanInv`/`mandarinInv`/`thaiInv`/`shanInv` abbrevs anchored to `Fragments.X.Definiteness.articleInventory`, and use those in all the existing inventory-bridge theorems (`inventory_agrees_with_params`, `inventory_derives_all_languages`, `shan_anaphoric_not_licensed_via_article`, etc.). New `mandarin_in_markedAnaphoric` makes the @cite{jenks-2018} backdrop visible. (6) **`Linglib.lean`** adds the four new fragment imports near each language's existing block. No semantic change; pure file relocation that brings the redesigned API into compliance with the strict `Theories → Fragments → Phenomena` dependency hierarchy. Build clean (988 jobs).

## [0.229.905] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Stage 7 of the Definiteness API redesign — Moroney2021 wired to `Core.Nominal`.** Verifies that the new `ArticleInventory` / `NominalKind` / `interpret` machinery reproduces and refines Moroney's typological analysis end-to-end, treating the existing study file as an integration test for the redesigned API. (1) **Thai inventory added.** `ArticleInventory.thai` (no indefinite, no unique, anaphoric-via-demonstrative, demonstrative paradigm, possessive paradigm) joins the language inventory; `thai_marking : ArticleInventory.thai.toMarkingStrategy = .markedAnaphoric` matches the §7 derivation. Same `markedAnaphoric` cell as Mandarin under Moroney's typology. (2) **`Moroney2021.lean` §14 added — "Integration with the Core.Nominal API".** Imports `Core.Nominal.{ArticleInventory,Description,Interpret}` and re-derives the four-language Table 4.4 typology from the inventory layer rather than the params layer: `inventory_agrees_with_params` proves `ArticleInventory.X.toMarkingParams = XParams` for English/German/Thai/Shan by `rfl`, witnessing that the inventory is the upstream object from which §7's `englishParams`/`germanParams`/`thaiParams`/`shanParams` fall out (no information loss for the params projection — the inventory carries strictly more data). `inventory_derives_all_languages` mirrors §7's `derive_all_languages` at the inventory level. (3) **Licensing predictions sharpen Moroney's central finding.** `shan_anaphoric_not_licensed_via_article` — Shan's `ArticleInventory.licensesKind` returns `false` for `.anaphoric` `NominalKind`s (no anaphoric article, no syncretic unique article either) — yet `shan_bare_licensed` and `shan_demonstrative_licensed` both return `true`, exposing the morphological substrate for Moroney's analysis: Shan expresses anaphoric definiteness through bare nouns + optional demonstratives, never through a dedicated article. The contrast with English (`english_anaphoric_licensed_via_syncretism` — *the* covers anaphoric via syncretism) and German (`german_anaphoric_licensed_via_strong_article` — independent strong article) makes the three modes of anaphoric licensing explicit at the inventory level. (4) **Interpretation agreement theorems for Shan.** `shan_bare_unique_agreement` is the Shan-named consequence of the universal `Core.Nominal.interpret_bare_eq_unique` — bare and uniqueness definites over the same restrictor select the same referent, the type-theoretic correlate of Moroney's claim that Shan bare nouns express weak/uniqueness definiteness via unblocked ι. `shan_demonstrative_anaphoric_agreement` is the Shan-named consequence of `interpret_demonstrative_eq_anaphoric` — *nâj*/*nân* and the bare anaphoric reading pick the same entity at a shared discourse index, formalizing Moroney's §2.1.3 finding that the demonstrative's deictic feature is a presupposition filter, not a referent selector. (5) **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` opens fixed.** `open Core.IntensionalLogic` and `open Core.IntensionalLogic.Variables` added at namespace top so the `Frame` and `DenotGS` references in `licensesKind` and `licensesKind_bare` resolve under `autoImplicit := false`. No new sorrys; no consumer-side changes outside the study file.

## [0.229.904] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Assignment` moved back to `Core` namespace (file-location revert of 0.229.899; wrapper-deletion preserved).** Stage 11 had pushed the polymorphic `Assignment α := ℕ → α` abbrev into `Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` to break a name clash with the now-deleted `Variables.Assignment F` wrapper. The clash is gone, and the IL-namespaced location was conceptually wrong: `Assignment` is **pre-intensional substrate** — pure Tarski variable mapping, used by extensional frameworks (plain DPL, classical model theory) just as much as by intensional ones — and didn't belong inside the intensional namespace. The intensional substrate (`Frame`, `SitAssignment F := Assignment F.Index`, `DenotGS`) builds on top of `Core.Assignment`, not the other way around. (1) **File:** `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Assignment.lean` deleted; the abbrev + `update` + `update_at` / `update_ne` / `update_overwrite` / `update_comm` / `update_self` lemmas are now at the top of `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` under `namespace Core` (alongside the `PartialAssign`, `PluralAssign`, `VarAssignment` abbrevs already there). The merged file is one logical module — variable assignments, total and partial. (2) **Consumers updated:** `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` (import `Linglib.Core.Assignment`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra.lean` (import + `open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/VarAssignment.lean` (drops the redundant IL import; `open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/DynamicSemantics.lean` (`open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/Core/CCP.lean` (import + `open _root_.Core (Assignment)` + re-export switched back: `export _root_.Core (Assignment)` and `export _root_.Core.Assignment (update update_at update_ne update_overwrite update_comm)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/DynamicGQ/UpdateTheoretic.lean` (`Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` → `Core.Assignment`, 9 occurrences). (3) **`Linglib.lean`:** drops the `import Linglib.Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` line. No semantic change; pure namespace move that aligns the file location with the conceptual layer (extensional substrate at top of `Core`, intensional substrate under `Core.IntensionalLogic`).

## [0.229.903] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/NullSubject.lean` mathlib-style hardening.** Five-part follow-up to 0.229.902. (1) **Prop + Decidable.** `satisfiesImplicational : ProDropProfile → Bool` is now `ProDropProfile.Satisfies : ProDropProfile → Prop` (`p.hasOvertPRO = true → p.allowsProDrop = false`) with a derived `Decidable` instance, so downstream theorems are closed by `decide` rather than `rfl` over the Boolean encoding. The Prop form composes with other logical predicates and matches the mathlib convention for universals. (2) **Contrapositive.** New `prodrop_excludes_overt_pro : p.Satisfies → p.allowsProDrop = true → p.hasOvertPRO = false` — the practically useful direction of the universal (given a *pro*-drop language, predict the absence of overt PRO). `Allotey2021.lean` exhibits the contrapositive concretely as `ga_prodrop_would_exclude_overt_pro`. (3) **Typology enum.** New `Core.NullSubject.Typology` enumerates the four cells (`nullPRO_noProDrop`, `nullPRO_proDrop`, `overtPRO_noProDrop`, `overtPRO_proDrop`); `Typology.isAttested` marks the fourth as forbidden; `ProDropProfile.classify` projects a profile to a cell; `satisfies_iff_attested` proves `p.Satisfies ↔ p.classify.isAttested = true`. The cells are now first-class objects rather than implicit Boolean tuples — makes "the forbidden cell" a named referent. `Ostrove2026.lean` adds `smpm_classified`, `english_classified`, `buli_classified` cell-by-cell theorems. (4) **`hasOvertPRO` widened to non-nullness.** `MinPronInventory.hasOvertPRO` now tests `controlForm != .null` rather than `== .pronoun`. The typological criterion @cite{ostrove-2026} states is overt-vs-null, not pronoun-vs-everything-else: an inventory whose controlled-subject form was `.reflexive` would equally count as overt PRO. All current inventories yield either `.null` or `.pronoun` so verdicts are unchanged; the widened definition is faithful to the stated criterion. (5) **Old API removed.** `satisfiesImplicational : Bool`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies` are gone — replaced by `Satisfies` + `null_pro_satisfies` + `overt_no_prodrop_satisfies` (Prop versions). The two consumer sites (`Allotey2021.lean`, `Ostrove2026.lean`) are updated to `by decide` proofs and the new lemma names; no other call sites existed.

## [0.229.902] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`ProDropProfile` lifted from `Phenomena/Control/` to `Core/NullSubject.lean`.** Pro-drop / overt-PRO is a typological parameter, not a control phenomenon: any syntactic framework must engage with it, and it is consumed by control studies but not specific to them. New `Core/NullSubject.lean` holds the framework-agnostic parameter (`ProDropProfile`) and @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal (`satisfiesImplicational`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies`) — zero theory dependencies, modeled on the existing `Core/SubjectProperties.lean`. The Minimalism-side bridge function (was `Phenomena.Control.hasOvertPRO : MinPronInventory PronForm → Bool`) is now `MinPronInventory.hasOvertPRO` in `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/MinimalPronoun.lean` § 3 (where `MinPronInventory` and `PronForm` already live), so the layer that depends on Minimalism stays inside Minimalism. `language : String` field dropped from `ProDropProfile` — Core types should not carry per-language metadata; the calling site (`gaProfile`, `smpmProfile`, etc.) is the natural anchor for the language label. `Studies/Allotey2021.lean` and `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` updated to `open Core.NullSubject` and use `inv.hasOvertPRO` dot-notation; `buliProfile` extracted as a top-level def in `Ostrove2026.lean` (was an inline structure literal). Old `Phenomena/Control/ProDropProfile.lean` deleted; `Linglib.lean` import line moved from the Phenomena block to the Core block.

## [0.229.901] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` — Stage 6 of the Definiteness API redesign.** Records a language's surface inventory of nominal forms as the upstream object from which the existing typology classifications fall out. (1) **`ArticleInventory` structure** with `hasIndefinite`, `hasUniqueArticle`, `hasAnaphoricArticle`, `uniqueAnaphoricSyncretism` (English *the* covers both vs German distinct weak/strong), `hasDemonstrative`, `hasPossessive`. Bare nominals are not recorded — every language allows them at some level; the question is what readings they admit, which is a matter of the type-shift hierarchy, not the article inventory. (2) **`licensesKind {F : Frame} (inv) : NominalKind F → Bool`** — pattern-match on the constructor: `.bare ↦ true` (universal); `.indefinite ↦ hasIndefinite`; `.unique ↦ hasUniqueArticle`; `.anaphoric ↦ hasAnaphoricArticle ∨ (hasUniqueArticle ∧ syncretism)` (English *the* counts via syncretism); `.demonstrative ↦ hasDemonstrative`; `.possessive ↦ hasPossessive`. The morphological-availability claim, not a grammaticality claim about every NP. `licensesKind_bare` is the universal bare-licensing simp lemma. (3) **Derived classifications.** `toMarkingParams : ArticleInventory → Core.Definiteness.DefMarkingParams` projects to the Moroney 3-boolean shape (the inventory carries strictly more information — demonstrative and possessive paradigms are discarded). `toMarkingStrategy := deriveStrategy ∘ toMarkingParams` and `toArticleType := strategyToArticleType ∘ toMarkingStrategy` re-use the existing derivation chain in `Core/Definiteness.lean` rather than restipulating it. (4) **Language instances** for English (syncretic *the*, full paradigm), German (distinct weak/strong, full paradigm), Mandarin (anaphoric-only, demonstrative + possessive), Shan (no articles, demonstrative + possessive). (5) **Consistency theorems.** `english_marking`, `german_marking`, `mandarin_marking`, `shan_marking` derive the four Moroney cells (`.generallyMarked`, `.bipartite`, `.markedAnaphoric`, `.unmarked`) from the four inventories by `rfl`. `english_mandarin_articleType_collapse` witnesses the lossiness of `ArticleType` relative to `DefMarkingStrategy`: distinct inventories with distinct strategies (`.generallyMarked` vs `.markedAnaphoric`) both collapse to `.weakOnly`; `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct` shows the inventories themselves are not collapsed, so the structure has finer resolution than either downstream typology. `Linglib.lean` import added. No existing call sites touched.

## [0.229.900] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Allotey 2021 cleanup pass.** Six-part rework of the 0.229.896 initial drop after a re-read of @cite{allotey-2021} surfaced inaccuracies and a build failure. (1) **ASCII identifier policy.** Lean 4 rejects IPA `ɛ` (U+025B) and `ŋ` (U+014B) as identifier characters; the original `Fragments/Ga/Basic.lean` and `Predicates.lean` did not compile. All constructor and definition names are now ASCII (`ake`, `keji`, `nye`, `kpleno`, `kpang`, `kee`, `finiteAke`, `finiteKeji`, `hiekpano`); IPA orthography lives in the `.form` strings only. Module docstring records the policy. (2) **Verb-list correction.** Replaced `kai` 'remember' (not in the paper) with `hiekpano` 'forget' (Allotey ex 37–38, the actual attested negative-implicative subject-control verb); dropped `susu` 'think' (not attested as Allotey CTP); kept `kee` 'say' as the standard `akɛ`-clause exemplar with a docstring noting it is the only entry not in Allotey's CTP list (kept so the OC-vs-non-OC contrast can be exhibited end-to-end). (3) **`ProDropProfile` factored out.** Moved `ProDropProfile`, `satisfiesImplicational`, `hasOvertPRO`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies` from `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` (where they were stranded) into a new top-level data file `Phenomena/Control/ProDropProfile.lean`. Both `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` and `Studies/Allotey2021.lean` import from there; the local re-statement in `Allotey2021.lean` is gone. (4) **Derive-don't-stipulate for OC.** `gaOCSignature` is now `if (clauseProperties c).noncoreferentialSubject then ocNone else ocFull` — derived from the noncoreferential-subject flag rather than stipulated per clause. `gaAgr` is now `(clauseProperties c).finiteComplementizer`, with bridge theorem `gaAgr_eq_complementizer_isFinite` connecting the Agr flag to the complementizer's finiteness via `complementizer_isFinite_eq_finiteFlag` (also new in `Basic.lean`). New universal `oc_iff_no_noncoreferential` makes the structural derivation visible. The previous `irrealisNi_is_OC : ... := rfl` is no longer a tautology over a stipulated mapping but follows from the derived definition. (5) **Irrealis-vs-subjunctive diagnostic.** New section adds `isSubjunctiveLike (c : EmbeddedClauseType) : Bool := (clauseProperties c).noncoreferentialSubject` (a clause type passes the subjunctive test iff it allows a noncoreferential embedded subject — the Romance obviation diagnostic). Three theorems: `irrealisNi_not_subjunctive`, `finiteAke_subjunctive_test_passes`, `finiteKeji_subjunctive_test_passes`. Formalizes Allotey's argument against the @cite{dakubu-2004} / @cite{campbell-2017} subjunctive analysis. The `gaToNoonan` mapping still sends `irrealisNi → .subjunctive` (Noonan's typological label, separate from the generative-grammar subjunctive); docstring records the distinction. (6) **Long Distance Agree analysis.** New `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/LongDistanceAgree.lean` formalizes @cite{szabolcsi-2009}'s three-condition LDA configuration (probe has valued φ, goal has unvalued φ, intervening C is non-defective) with `LDAConfig.licenses` and three structural impossibility theorems (`blocking_c_defeats_LDA`, `valued_goal_has_no_LDA_target`, `unvalued_probe_cannot_LDA`). `Allotey2021.lean` now wires `gaLDAConfig`, `ga_satisfies_LDA`, and `ga_LDA_implies_OC` to make Allotey's actual analytical contribution (LDA-driven cross-clausal valuation of the embedded minimal pronoun) visible — previously the formalization plugged Allotey only into the @cite{ostrove-2026} minimal-pronoun typology. **Bib:** added stubs for `szabolcsi-2009`, `satik-2019`, `dakubu-2004`, `campbell-2017` (all with `note` fields flagging fields that were not verifiable from training data). `Linglib.lean` adds `Phenomena.Control.ProDropProfile` and `Theories.Syntax.Minimalism.LongDistanceAgree` imports.

## [0.229.899] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Assignment` consolidated into `Core.IntensionalLogic`.** The polymorphic `Assignment E := ℕ → E` abbrev (and its `update` / `update_at` / `update_ne` / `update_overwrite` / `update_comm` / `update_self` lemmas) move from `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` to a new `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Assignment.lean` and live under the `Core.IntensionalLogic` namespace. The redundant `abbrev Assignment (F : Frame) := Core.Assignment F.Entity` wrapper in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` is dropped; entity-pronoun signatures now read `Assignment F.Entity` directly, situation-pronoun signatures keep `SitAssignment F`. Motivation: the wrapper had the same name as the polymorphic abbrev but a different arity (`Frame` vs `Type*`), which silently lost name resolution against `open Core.IntensionalLogic` and forced fully-qualified `Variables.Assignment F` everywhere in `Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean` (Stage 5 of the Definiteness redesign). With both names unified to `Assignment` over `Type*`, `Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean` drops the qualifier. Updated callers: `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` (now hosts only `PartialAssign` / `PluralAssign` / `VarAssignment`, imports the new IL/Assignment), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra.lean` and `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/VarAssignment.lean` and `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/DynamicSemantics.lean` (`open Core (Assignment)` → `open Core.IntensionalLogic (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/Core/CCP.lean` (re-export switched to the IL namespace), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/DynamicGQ/UpdateTheoretic.lean` (`Core.Assignment` → `Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` qualifier, 5 occurrences), `Linglib.lean` import added. No semantic change; pure namespace move. Stops the clash that bit Stage 5 and unblocks Stage 6.

## [0.229.898] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR J (interrogative completes the 3×3 Portner table).** Three coordinated additions wire interrogative force into the Mood substrate so that the @cite{portner-2018} unification table gains its third column. (1) **`Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`** — `structure POSWQ extends POSW` adds an `inquiry : Setoid W` (the QUD-induced answer partition; `⊤` = no question). The third sentence-mood update `POSWQ.inquire c q := { c with inquiry := c.inquiry ⊓ q }` is the partition-side analogue of `POSW.plus` (refining `cs`) and `POSW.star` (refining `lt`); `inquire_targets_disjoint_components` proves the partition operation leaves both `cs` and `lt` untouched (the third leg of @cite{portner-2018}'s thesis that mood updates target disjoint substrate components). The third necessity modal `POSWQ.boxAns c p := ∀ w v, c.cs w → c.cs v → c.inquiry.r w v → (p w ↔ p v)` says `p` has constant truth value within each inquiry cell — informational answerhood, the modal counterpart of `POSW.boxCs`/`boxLt`. The refinement preorder lifts componentwise (`Preorder (POSWQ W)`); `inquire_le_self`, `inquire_mono`, and `boxAns_anti` mirror the POSW preorder layer. `boxAns` is *not* upward-monotone in `p` (a strengthening can break per-cell constancy), but it *is* closed under negation (`boxAns_not`) and conjunction (`boxAns_and`) — answers compose like ordinary propositions. Concrete two-cell separation witness `sepPOSWQ` proves `boxAns_not_reducible_to_boxCs`: there exists a POSWQ where `boxAns p` holds but `boxCs p` fails, so the inquiry component is doing genuine work. (2) **`Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean` POSWQ bridge.** `polarSetoid q : Setoid W` (two worlds equivalent iff they agree on `q`) is the smallest piece of partition structure a single yes/no question contributes; `qudInquiry K : Setoid W` folds the QUD stack as iterated meet of polar Setoids (with `⊤` as identity); `toPOSWQ K : POSWQ W := { K.toPOSW with inquiry := K.qudInquiry }` projects the scoreboard into the POSWQ substrate. Bridge theorem `toPOSWQ_interrogation_eq_inquire` proves `interrogationUpdate` is `POSWQ.inquire (polarSetoid q)` on the inquiry side — definitional, by the foldr convention chosen so the cons reduces to right-meet. Corollary `boxAns_polar_after_interrogation`: after posing `q`, the polar partition of `q` itself is settled by the resulting POSWQ. The three discourse-update bridges now form a complete table: `assertionUpdate` ↔ `plus` ↔ `boxCs_after_assertion`; `directionUpdate` ↔ `star` ↔ `direction_demotes_violators`; `interrogationUpdate` ↔ `inquire` ↔ `boxAns_polar_after_interrogation`. (3) **`Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean` extended.** `VerbalMoodOp` gains a third constructor `.interrogative`; `interp` lifts to `POSWQ W → (W → Prop) → Prop` with cases `.indicative ↦ c.toPOSW.boxCs`, `.subjunctive ↦ c.toPOSW.boxLt`, `.interrogative ↦ c.boxAns`. The previous uniform `interp_mono` is replaced by `interp_indicative_mono` and `interp_subjunctive_mono` (each delegating to the corresponding `POSW.box*_mono`); `boxAns` has no upward monotonicity in `p`, and the asymmetry is itself part of the unification's content (different operators, different ordering behaviors, different lattice substrates). Separation theorems: `indicative_ne_subjunctive` (lifted from PR I via `POSWQ.ofPOSW sepPOSW`); `interrogative_ne_indicative` (delegates to `POSWQ.boxAns_not_reducible_to_boxCs`). `MoodSelector.toVerbalMood` is left unchanged (declarative-complement classification doesn't include question-embedders); `toVerbalMood_ne_interrogative` makes the closure explicit — the `.interrogative` case is reachable by direct construction, not by `MoodSelector` projection. The completed Portner unification:

| layer            | declarative                  | imperative                    | interrogative              |
|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------|
| sentence mood    | assertion (`+`-update on cs) | direction (`⋆`-update on lt)  | interrogation (`?` on inq) |
| modal necessity  | `boxCs` (informational)      | `boxLt` (preferential)        | `boxAns` (answerhood)      |
| verbal mood      | `interp .indicative`         | `interp .subjunctive`         | `interp .interrogative`    |

Three components (`cs`, `lt`, `inquiry`), three update operations, three necessity modals, three verbal-mood operators — all type-driven on a single substrate. Closes the 7-PR Mood API unification arc. **Refactor:** the standalone `Core/Mood/Refinement.lean` from the prep PR is folded into `Core/Mood/POSW.lean` §4 (refinement preorder is intrinsic to POSW; mathlib pattern: small natural orders live with the structure). `Mathlib.Order.Basic` is now imported by `POSW.lean`. `Core/Mood.lean` re-exports `POSWQ`. Build: 0 new sorrys; existing pre-PR sorry warnings unaffected.

## [0.229.897] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean`.** The interpretation function `interpret : NominalKind F → Variables.Assignment F → Variables.SitAssignment F → Option F.Entity`, mapping the `NominalKind` sum to a referent (or `none` for presupposition failure / inapplicability) by composing the maximality/uniqueness operators in `Core/Nominal/Maximality.lean`. Per-constructor reductions: `bare R` and `unique R _` ↦ `russellIota (R g gs ·)` (default Core reading is weak/uniqueness; mereologically-aware fragments can opt into `sharvyMax` separately); `indefinite _` ↦ `none` (existential closure isn't an entity at this layer); `anaphoric R d` ↦ `some (g d)` if `R g gs (g d)` else `none`; `demonstrative R _ _ d` ↦ same as anaphoric (deictic feature is a presupposition filter, not a selector); `possessive R poss rel` ↦ `russellIota` over the restrictor conjoined with the possession relation. Six per-constructor `@[simp]` reductions plus three cross-constructor agreement theorems: `interpret_bare_eq_unique` (Core defaults bare to weak/uniqueness), `interpret_demonstrative_eq_anaphoric` (deictic doesn't change referent), `interpret_unique_index_irrelevant` (the situation-pronoun index records *which* pronoun is bound, not what the operator computes — the actual situation comes via the `SitAssignment`). Three witness theorems: `interpret_unique_witness_satisfies` (whatever the operator returns satisfies the restrictor), `interpret_anaphoric_witness_is_indexed` (anaphoric never picks anything other than `g d`), `interpret_anaphoric_isSome_iff` (anaphoric succeeds iff the restrictor accepts the indexed entity). All proofs use `Classical.dec` locally via `letI`. `Linglib.lean` import added.

## [0.229.896] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Allotey 2021 (Gã obligatory control) formalized.** Three new files: (1) `Linglib/Fragments/Ga/Basic.lean` — Gã (Kwa, Niger-Congo) fragment: pronoun proclitic paradigm (mi/o/e/wɔ/nyɛ/amɛ × sg/pl), TAM (`baa-` future, `mii-` progressive, `é-` perfect, `á` irrealis tone), three complementizers (`akɛ`, `kɛji`, `ni`) with `Complementizer.isFinite` (`ni` = irrealis), three-way `EmbeddedClauseType` (`finiteAkɛ | finiteKɛji | irrealisNi`) with `ClauseProperties` (unrestrictedTAM, noncoreferentialSubject, finiteComplementizer). (2) `Linglib/Fragments/Ga/Predicates.lean` — Gã CTPs with Landau predicate class: subject control `tao` 'want', `kplɛnɔ` 'agree', `kpaŋ` 'plan' (desiderative), `kai` 'remember', `nyɛ` 'manage' (implicative); object control `kenya` 'urge', `laka` 'persuade' (desiderative), `dai` 'force' (implicative), `wa` 'help' (unclassified); finite complement `kɛɛ` 'say', `susu` 'think' (propositional). `CTP.controlTier` derives Landau tier per verb. (3) `Linglib/Phenomena/Control/Studies/Allotey2021.lean` — `gaOCSignature : EmbeddedClauseType → OCSignature` (irrealisNi → ocFull, finite* → ocNone) with per-clause-type `isOC` theorems by `rfl`; `landau_predicts_control` (Landau cSubjunctive/finite + Agr → Gã OC pattern) by `cases <;> rfl`; per-verb OC theorems for all CTPs in the inventory; `oc_determined_by_clause_type` universal (any CTP whose complement is irrealis shows OC) by `cases <;> rfl`; `gaInventory : MinPronInventory PronForm` with `ga_overt_pro` and `ga_has_reflexive`; pro-drop / overt-PRO universal application (`gaProfile`, `ga_satisfies_universal`); Noonan complement-type bridge `gaToNoonan`. The Pollock 1989 V-movement parallelism in Allotey is deliberately not formalized — Pollock's diagnostic requires a free Neg head (Spec-NegP) and Gã `-ee/-ko` is suffixal, so the inference doesn't license without independent morphological argumentation outside the paper. Removed stranded `gaInventory`/`ga_overt_pro`/`ga_satisfies_universal` from `Ostrove2026.lean` (relocated to their proper home in the Gã-focused study). Bib entry `allotey-2021` upgraded role: cited → formalized.

## [0.229.895] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Maximality.lean` — Sharvy/Križ + Coppock–Beaver operators** — Stage 4 of the Definiteness API redesign. Predicate operators over `F.Denot .et` for Stage 5 to compose. (1) **Coppock–Beaver factorization** (@cite{coppock-beaver-2015}): `Existence P := ∃x, P x` and `Uniqueness P := ∀x y, P x → P y → x = y` as independent predicates; `existsUnique := Existence ∧ Uniqueness` is the classical Russellian condition; `existsUnique_iff_existence_and_uniqueness` is `Iff.rfl` (by construction); `uniqueness_does_not_imply_existence` proves the empty predicate satisfies vacuous Uniqueness without Existence — the data point that motivates @cite{coppock-beaver-2015}'s split. (2) **Russellian iota** (order-free): `russellIota P : Option F.Entity` returns the unique witness when `existsUnique P`; three theorems wire it together (`russellIota_isSome_iff_existsUnique`, `russellIota_witness_satisfies`, `russellIota_witness_unique`). (3) **Sharvy maximality** (@cite{sharvy-1980}): `IsMaximal P e := P e ∧ ∀ x, P x → x ≤ e` and the selector `sharvyMax P : Option F.Entity` over `[PartialOrder F.Entity]` — collapses to Russellian iota on flat orders, returns the @cite{link-1983} sum on join semilattices for plurals; `isMaximal_unique` shows antisymmetry collapses two maxima into one. (4) **Križ homogeneity** (@cite{kriz-2015}): `Homogeneous P S := (∀ x, P x → S x) ∨ (∀ x, P x → ¬ S x)` — the predicate that licenses uniform-truth-or-uniform-falsehood for plural definites; `uniqueness_implies_homogeneous_classical` proves Russellian uniqueness vacuously implies homogeneity (the singular-definite limiting case). All proofs use `Classical.dec` locally via `letI` — no `native_decide` and no `noncomputable` leakage outside the maximality module. The interpretation function in Stage 5 will compose these operators with the `NominalKind` sum from Stage 3 and the `DenotGS` substrate from Stage 1. No existing call sites touched.

## [0.229.894] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Faust 2026: universal Faust+Kramer integration theorem.** New `intrusion_wellformed_iff_no_intruder_or_n_with_gen` in §12 of `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean`: for any `RootTemplateMatch m` and any `CatHead ch`, `m.intrusionLicensed ch.licensesIntrusion = true ↔ m.hasIntruder = false ∨ (ch.cat = .n ∧ ch.phi.gender.isSome = true)`. Universally-quantified statement of @cite{faust-2026} (11), derived by composing `intrusionLicensed_iff` (Templates.lean) with `licensesIntrusion_iff_n_and_gen` (Categorizer.lean) — no new computational content; the previously per-instance integration of @cite{faust-2026}'s licensing predicate with @cite{kramer-2020}'s `CatHead` taxonomy is now structural. Two corollaries make the verbal/nominal split explicit: `v_plain_licenses_iff_no_intruder` (under v_plain, licensing reduces to intruder-freeness) and `n_uFem_licenses_universally` (under n_uFem, every match licensed regardless of intruder status). Subsumes the 12 per-derivation `decide` theorems (kept as a per-datum verification roll-call) and the 6-conjunct `verbal_nominal_asymmetry_from_kramer` (kept as the empirical-claim digest); the new theorem is the single structural fact behind both.

## [0.229.893] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Description.lean` — `NominalKind F` sum type** — Stage 3 of the Definiteness API redesign. A single sum type, parameterized by a `Core.IntensionalLogic.Frame`, covers the principal flavors of nominal description that the syntax–semantics interface needs to distinguish: `bare` (number-neutral; covert type-shifts decide the reading), `indefinite` (∃; introduces a new discourse referent), `unique` (Coppock–Beaver weak/uniqueness; Sharvy/Križ maximal — carries a `situationIdx` for the Hanink-bound resource situation), `anaphoric` (Schwarz strong-article / Hanink-indexed familiarity — carries a `discourseIdx`), `demonstrative` (Patel-Grosz–Grosz/Davis: a separate object, not "strong the" — carries a `Core.Deixis.Feature` deictic, a situation index, and a discourse index), and `possessive` (definite via a possession relation; carries restrictor + possessor + relation). Every restrictor has type `DenotGS F .et`, the bi-assignment-relative type added in Stage 1 — both entity assignments and situation assignments are first-class arguments, the @cite{hanink-2021}/@cite{bondarenko-2023} position. Four classification predicates supplied (`isDefinite`, `isAnaphoric`, `usesSituationPronoun`, `expectedPresupType`); two structural theorems wire them together: `isDefinite_iff_expectedPresup_some` and `isAnaphoric_implies_familiarity`. No semantics yet — Stage 4 supplies maximality + Coppock–Beaver uniqueness operators, Stage 5 supplies the interpretation `NominalKind F → DenotGS F .e`. No existing call sites touched. Reuses Stage 2's `Core.Deixis.Feature` for demonstratives.

## [0.229.892] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR I (verbal mood as POSW component selection).** New `Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean` formalizes @cite{portner-2018} (Ch. 2)'s reduction of the indicative/subjunctive contrast to *which POSW component the embedded clause is evaluated against*. `VerbalMoodOp := indicative | subjunctive`, with `interp : VerbalMoodOp → POSW W → (W → Prop) → Prop` defined as `.indicative ↦ POSW.boxCs` and `.subjunctive ↦ POSW.boxLt` — type-driven, no auxiliary state. The Truth intuition (Farkas 1985, Portner 1997 tradition) gets `boxCs` (universal quantification over the modal base / informational component); the Comparison intuition (Giorgi & Pianesi 1997 tradition) gets `boxLt` (quantification over the best-ranked subset / preferential component). Theorems: `interp_indicative` / `interp_subjunctive` (definitional simp lemmas), `interp_mono` (uniform upward monotonicity across the Truth/Comparison split), `indicative_ne_subjunctive` (concrete two-world `Bool` POSW where `boxLt sepProp` holds but `boxCs sepProp` fails — the type-level reflex of Portner's observation that comparative-meaning predicates entail truth only at the best-ranked subset, not the entire modal base). The `MoodSelector` enum (Mood/Basic.lean) projects to `VerbalMoodOp` via `MoodSelector.toVerbalMood`: `.indicativeSelecting ↦ some .indicative`, `.subjunctiveSelecting ↦ some .subjunctive`, variable/neutral ↦ `none` (their selection pattern is not committed to a single POSW component by lexical class alone). `prefersSubjunctive_eq_subjunctive` proves the existing `prefersSubjunctive` boolean and `toVerbalMood` agree by construction — two surface representations of the same lexical-class commitment.

The full Portner unification is now visible at the type level across the three mood layers:

| layer            | indicative-flavored          | subjunctive-flavored          |
|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| sentence mood    | assertion (`+`-update on cs) | direction (`⋆`-update on lt)  |
| modal necessity  | `boxCs` (informational)      | `boxLt` (preferential)        |
| verbal mood      | `interp .indicative`         | `interp .subjunctive`         |

The shared substrate is `POSW`. The previous PRs (E.5–H) wired the bottom two rows; PR I closes the top of the table by reading the modal row as a complementizer-domain selector triggered by the matrix predicate's lexical class.

## [0.229.891] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Promote `SpatialRelation` to `Core/Deixis/Feature.lean`** — Stage 2 of the Definiteness API redesign. The two-constructor `SpatialRelation` enum (`proximal | distal`) that previously lived locally inside `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` is now a framework-agnostic `Core.Deixis.Feature` enum with four constructors (`proximal | medial | distal | unspecified`), covering the WALS Ch. 41 distance-system typology: two-way systems (~54% of attested languages: English, Mandarin, French, Shan, Magahi, most IE languages); three-way distance-oriented systems (Latin, Spanish, Hunzib) via `medial`; distance-neutral demonstratives (Modern German *dieser*, Hawaiian no-contrast forms) via `unspecified`. The file documents that adding finer granularity (person-orientation for Japanese ko/so/a, Korean i/ku/ce; visibility for Quechua, Kwakwaka'wakw, ASL; elevation for Dyirbal, Nepali) should follow the *concrete-then-abstract* discipline — add a constructor when a fragment actually needs it, not in anticipation. Auxiliary `Feature.encodesDistance : Feature → Bool` (returns `true` iff non-`unspecified`) with a one-line iff characterization. `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` migrated: `ShanDemonstrative.spatial : Core.Deixis.Feature` (was `SpatialRelation`); `naj`/`nan` constructors unchanged (constructor names `.proximal`/`.distal` preserved); `demDenotation`, `dem_is_anaphoric`, `dem_refines_bare` signatures updated to take `Core.Deixis.Feature → E → Bool` spatial predicates. `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` follows: `open Core.Deixis (Feature)`; one theorem signature retypes to `Feature → E → Bool`. `Linglib.lean` adds `import Linglib.Core.Deixis.Feature`. The unrelated `SpatialRelation` enum inside `Phenomena/Evidentiality/Studies/Koev2017.lean` (`samePlace | differentPlace`, an event-locality predicate for evidentials, not a deictic feature) is a namespacing-distinct local type and is unaffected.

## [0.229.890] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Faust 2026 mathlib-PR-review pass.** Six fixes to `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean` and its bridges. (1) Fixed docstring hallucination on `hebrew_kala_unfilled` — replaced reference to nonexistent `hebrew_kala_unfilled_but_misalign_safe` with the actual joint statement `qataT_qata_three_way_contrast`. (2/3) Replaced 5 bare-`simp` invocations (lines 711, 753, 763, 788, 1070) with `decide` or `simp only [...]` per CLAUDE.md preference. (4) Promoted `Root.satisfiesOCP` from study-local def to `Core/Morphology/ConsonantalRoot.lean` (alongside a new auxiliary `Root.adjDupCount` — segment-level OCP infrastructure that other templatic-morphology studies will want; theory-neutral and not coupled to the tier-projection-level `Phonology.Constraints.adjacentIdentical`). (5) Moved `CatHead.licensesIntrusion` from `_root_`-qualified study-local def to its natural home in `Theories/Morphology/DM/Categorizer.lean` (alongside `satisfiesLicense` and the Kramer taxonomy it ranges over), with the 6 per-canonical-head verification theorems (`n_uFem_licenses_intrusion`, `v_plain_blocks_intrusion`, etc.) and the iff characterization `licensesIntrusion_iff_n_and_gen` migrating with it — these test the predicate's behavior against canonical `CatHead`s, not Faust-specific data. ## [0.229.889] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Situation-pronoun substrate in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean`** — Stage 1 of the Definiteness API redesign. Adds `SitAssignment F := Core.Assignment F.Index` (the type-level dual of the entity `Assignment F := Core.Assignment F.Entity`), `interpSitPronoun n gs := gs n` (parallel to `interpPronoun`), `DenotGS F ty := Assignment F → SitAssignment F → F.Denot ty` (bi-assignment-relative denotations), and lift constructors `DenotGS.ofDenotG` / `DenotGS.const`. Reuses `Core.Assignment` at type `F.Index` so all update lemmas (`update_at`, `update_ne`, `update_overwrite`, `update_comm`, `update_self`) come for free at the new instantiation. The trivial `DenotGS.ofDenotG_const` agreement theorem documents that the two lift paths from a frame-level constant coincide. No existing call sites touched; no theorems weakened. Sets up Hanink (2018, 2021) / Bondarenko (2022, 2023) situation-pronoun binding to be used by the upcoming `NominalKind` sum (Stage 3) and `interpret` function (Stage 5) of the Definiteness redesign.

## [0.229.888] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR H (`directionUpdate ↔ POSW.star`).** Completes the 7-PR arc by wiring the directive side of the Portner 2018 unification thesis. Three additions to `Linglib/Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`: (1) lifted the inline `let rec go` inside `directionUpdate` to a top-level `addGoalAt : List (GoalSet W) → Nat → Nat → Goal W → List (GoalSet W)` helper (out-of-range targets silently leave the list unchanged — that's a stipulated property of the discourse update, not of POSW). (2) `Scoreboard.goalContents := K.goals.flatMap GoalSet.toPropertyList` flattens per-agent goal sets into a single property list — the substrate of the goal-induced preference ordering. (3) `Scoreboard.toPOSW` no longer carries a trivial `lt`; it now derives it from `goalContents`: `lt w v := ∀ p ∈ K.goalContents, p v → p w` (`w` is at least as good as `v` iff `w` satisfies every goal `v` satisfies). Reflexivity and transitivity follow trivially from chaining implications. The bridge theorem `toPOSW_direction_eq_star` proves `(K.directionUpdate p s t pr).toPOSW.lt w v ↔ (K.toPOSW.star p).lt w v` under the precondition `t < K.goals.length`, mirroring the assertion-side bridge from PR G — the proof goes through `mem_addGoalAt_flatMap` (the new directive's content is simply added to the goal-content list, with everything else preserved) and `mem_directionUpdate_goalContents`. Corollary `direction_demotes_violators` proves that after directing `p`, no `p`-violator can dominate a `p`-satisfier in the goal-induced ordering — the `lt`-side analogue of `boxCs_after_assertion`. The `lt` refactor required updating the simp lemma `toPOSW_lt` (the trivial `↔ True` is replaced by the goal-quantifier expansion); `toPOSW_cs` and `toPOSW_assertion_eq_plus` are unchanged because the assertion update doesn't touch `K.goals` (`assertion_preserves_goalContents := rfl`).

The two scoreboard updates that target the POSW substrate now correspond, by construction, to the two POSW updates of @cite{portner-2018}:

| scoreboard      | POSW    | informational consequence                  |
|-----------------|---------|--------------------------------------------|
| assertionUpdate | `plus`  | `boxCs_plus_self` (Stalnakerian principle) |
| directionUpdate | `star`  | `lt`-refinement: p-violators demoted       |

Out of scope for this PR (genuinely beyond Portner's two-update substrate): an unconditional `boxLt p` analogue for direction (Portner's `⋆`-update doesn't make `p` preferentially necessary without an "anchor" assumption — the well-known asymmetry between `+` and `⋆`); interrogation update wiring (a third POSW update mode beyond `+`/`⋆` would be a new arc, not a completion of this one).

## [0.229.887] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`HarmonySystem` now structurally contains its `TierRule`** — the bridge architecture from 0.229.884 (parallel `triggerValue` + `toTierRule` + `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` proving them equivalent under blocker-free, transparent-trigger conditions) is replaced by structural containment. The new `Theories/Phonology/Harmony/Defs.lean` defines `HarmonySystem` as a record carrying a `rule : Phonology.Alternation.TierRule Segment` (the formal core — @cite{belth-2026}'s tier-based AGREE rule, the central object in modern computational/learnability phonology) plus `feature : Feature`, `isTarget : Segment → Bool`, and `isBlocker : Segment → Bool` (the @cite{rose-walker-2011} typological "spreading discipline" layered on top). `triggerValue` is now defined as `sys.rule.apply` dispatched on `harmonyDomain sys stem` — *true by construction*, no bridge theorem, no disjointness side-conditions, no possibility of the typological wrapper and the formal rule silently disagreeing.
- **`HarmonySystem.mk'` smart constructor** exposes the @cite{rose-walker-2011} six-way decomposition (feature / isTrigger / isTarget / isTransparent / direction / isBlocker) as named arguments; the constructor compiles these into the underlying `TierRule` (tier projects out transparent segments; side derives from direction; context class is the trigger predicate; relation is `.agree`; featureValue extracts `s.spec feature`). This is the public construction API — direct positional record syntax (`{ feature := ..., isTrigger := ..., ... }`) no longer works, since `isTrigger`/`isTransparent`/`direction` are no longer top-level fields. Migration: Turkish (`palatalHarmony`, `labialHarmony`), Finnish (`finnishHarmony`), Hungarian (`hungarianPalatalHarmony`, `hungarianLabialHarmony`, `palatalHarmony_eBlocks`) all switched to `HarmonySystem.mk'`.
- **`HarmonyDir.toSide` bridge** (`HarmonyDir → Phonology.Alternation.Side`): rightward / bidirectional ↦ `.left` (context precedes the suffix slot); leftward ↦ `.right`. Bidirectional collapses to rightward operationally (the same `harmonyDomain`/`triggerValue` computation handles both); the typological distinction lives only at the `mk'` argument site.
- **Convenience accessor** `Phonology.Harmony.isTrigger sys s := sys.rule.targetIsContext s` recovers the Rose-Walker trigger field from the underlying rule.
- **Deleted**: `HarmonySystem.toTierRule` (now redundant — `sys.rule` IS the TierRule), `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` bridge theorem (true-by-construction now), `filter_through_byClass` private lemma (was only there to support the bridge), and the `direction : HarmonyDir` field on `HarmonySystem` (encoded in `rule.side`; preserved as a smart-constructor argument only). One Hungarian fragment theorem (`hungarian_finnish_same_direction`) restated in terms of `rule.side = .left` rather than the deleted `direction` field.
- **`Theories/Phonology/Alternation.lean` docstring** updated: the wrapping relationship is now described as "Rose-Walker harmony systems structurally contain a `TierRule` as their value-prediction core" (replacing "wrapped via `HarmonySystem.toTierRule`"). The architectural commitment is now visible in the type, not deferred to a bridge theorem.

### Why
The bridge-theorem architecture from 0.229.884 left the relationship between Rose-Walker harmony and Belth-style tier rules implicit: two parallel implementations, glued by an equivalence proof that only held under restrictive hypotheses (no blockers, triggers non-transparent, rightward direction). That dodged exactly the cases where the typology actually does work — blockers are the whole point of opaque vs. transparent. The structural containment matches the field's center of gravity: in the modern computational/learnability framing (Heinz/Jardine/Chandlee TSL, @cite{belth-2026}), tier projection plus a local rule on it is the formal primitive; the @cite{rose-walker-2011} trigger/target/transparent/blocker/direction decomposition is the typological lens for cataloguing what languages do, not a separate formal object. So `HarmonySystem` *contains* a `TierRule` (descriptive wrapper around formal core) rather than *bridging to* one (peer relationship via equivalence).

## [0.229.886] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR G (POSW substrate bridge for `Scoreboard`).** `Linglib/Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean` gains a `toPOSW : Scoreboard W → Core.Mood.POSW W` projection: `cs := K.contextSet`, `lt := fun _ _ => True` (trivial preference ordering — directive update via `POSW.star` deferred). Two simp lemmas (`toPOSW_cs`, `toPOSW_lt`) characterize the projection. The Stalnakerian assertion principle ("asserting `p` makes `p` informationally necessary") is derived from `Core.Mood.POSW.boxCs_plus_self` via the bridge: `toPOSW_assertion_eq_plus` proves `(K.assertionUpdate p a).toPOSW.cs = (K.toPOSW.plus p).cs` (one-line `simp` + `And.comm` — the assertion update *is* POSW's `plus` after projecting); `boxCs_after_assertion` then chains them to derive `(K.assertionUpdate p a).toPOSW.boxCs p`. The CG storage stays as `List (W → Prop)` (move-tracking history is preserved); only the projected POSW substrate is exposed for properties about the context set itself. This completes the 7-PR mood-API unification arc started in PR A; the remaining gap is wiring directive updates through `POSW.star`, which would require deriving a preference ordering from goals (deferred — a substantive Cohen-style modeling decision rather than mechanical bridging).

## [0.229.885] - 2026-04-18
